name: ATV
creator: Badimo
description: The ATV is a vehicle that was released in 2017 Winter Update of December 23, 2017. It is the fourth slowest land vehicle in the game in terms of top speed, only ahead of the Crew Capsule, Tank and Sloop. The ATV is a four-wheeled vehicle in Jailbreak, mainly designed for off-road travel, being tolerant of rough terrain. This vehicle drives well on any kind of terrain, and in addition, it has decent handling. When the ATV was first released, it sparked many complaints about its performance. The vehicle only has one spawn located at the Mini Train Station - where players are teleported if they fail to leave the Cargo Train before it enters the tunnel, making it a good getaway vehicle. It also had 2 seats, but would constantly fling around. To fix this, Badimo removed one seat and nerfed its performance. It has very low performance, making it one of the least-used vehicles in the game.

name: Agent
creator: Zewo_Tsu (102700598)
description: The Agent was available to purchase for only six days before becoming unobtainable on May 7, 2021. It could have been bought at the central intersection in Rising City. The Agent is known for its extremely limited obtainability and the equipment of missiles on the vehicle.  The Agent is unique for being a weaponized car, featuring a pair of missile launchers on the front fenders. They can be aimed properly if the driver is skilled, simply firing in the direction the car is facing Deals a maximum of 60 damage to vehicles per shot. They are weaker than other vehicle missiles, for killing players they arent effective. But for disabling vehicles it is significantly better, most people do not prefer the missiles on the Agent, and instead use missiles on aerial vehicles such as the Black Hawk and the Jet.

name: Airtail
creator: Badimo
description: The Airtail is a hypercar vehicle in Jailbreak, and was added in the March 2021 Mid-Season Update of March 6th, 2021 to replace the retired Megalodon. It spawned at the runway of the Bounty Bay Airport, and, for a short time, the Military Base. It was retired on January 9th, 2022. The Airtail is well-known for its remarkable performance and is commonly compared to the Roadster, M12 Molten, and the Eclaire by the community. The Airtail went off-sale on January 7th, 2022, along with the Classic as a part of Museum Revamp Update; both went on-sale again on January 8th, 2022 only in new servers, due to a data rollback to users who were involved with an infinite money glitch that was patched. It went off-sale again on January 9th, 2022, making this vehicle unobtainable. The Airtail has notable butterfly doors, similar to the Beam Hybrid and Drone. The vehicle also has a long "tail" due to its rear end design, hence its name. It is a three-seater, which is uncommon, as most other hypercars and supercars are two-seaters. This makes the Airtail a great vehicle to use for trios.

name: Ambulance
creator: Badimo
description: The Ambulance has 2 seats and a stretcher in the rear of the vehicle, which heals players at a rate of 10 health per second. The doors to the stretcher can be opened by pressing "G", pressing the GUI button on mobile, or selecting the GUI button on console. When any player enters the Ambulance's driver's seat, sirens will automatically be equipped, no matter what team the player is on. The sirens also have a unique sound, different from the police siren sound. On November 3rd, 2022, the Ambulance was revamped as part of the 5 Days of Vehicles update, with a whole new detail and a free upgrade for existing owners. Unlike most other vehicles, the Ambulance has a special ability. If players sit on the stretcher in the back of the Ambulance, they will gain 10 health per second. The doors can be opened by the driver of the vehicle by pressing the "G" button. The doors do not need to be opened to enter or exit the stretcher. Players do not need to own the Ambulance to use the stretcher either, as they can simply walk up to any unlocked Ambulance, sit in the stretcher, and gain health.

name: Aperture
creator: Stealth_Llama (424239986)
description: The Aperture is a limited time vehicle in Jailbreak. This hypercar features a V12 engine sound and a trailing taillight effect. It was available for 7 days. It can only be obtained through trading. The vehicle notably has a very sharp and jagged body shape, creating a standout design with cross-shaped headlights similar to that of the Beignet's and six-pointed star-like taillights with a red trail effect when braking, similar to that of the Shogun. Additionally, the Aperture has a unique V12 engine sound. It can carry two players - a driver and a single passenger.

name: Arachnid
creator: Badimo
description: The Arachnid is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It required reaching level 30 in Season 1 and level 5 in Season 2 on the Police team. The Arachnid is based on the Batmobile seen in "The Dark Knight" movie trilogy, also called the Tumbler. It was the police team vehicle prize of OG Seasons 1 and 2, alongside its counterpart: the Torpedo. It spawned inside of the Secret Agent Base alongside a Camaro, where it stayed for about 6 months before being replaced by the Raptor. Some players argue that this vehicle was easier to obtain than its Criminal counterpart: the Torpedo. Both were equal in price and level requirements, but police levels were considered easier to earn. This is because police gain XP for the simple task of arresting, while in comparison, criminals need to complete somewhat long heists to earn XP. Throughout its attainability, the Arachnid had a very mediocre top speed, making many people prefer to aim towards buying the Torpedo instead. In July of 2022, the car received a buff to be able to overdrive from 168 MPH all the way to 236 MPH, which is faster than the Volt, Roadster, Banana Car and many other vehicles.

name: Badger
creator: RallySubbie (71744786)
description: The Badger is a vehicle that was released in Jailbreak on February 4, 2022, along with Season 8: Looking to the Future. It spawns by the right exit of the Museum and at the beach near Crater City Bank. The Badger is based off the sixth generation of the Ford Bronco. As described by Badimo, it is "a fun two-seat offroader". The vxhicle's most notable points in terms of appearance are its distinct front headlights and lightbar, the snorkel, and the two suitcases strapped onto its roof rack. One of the suitcases is colored blue, while the other is colored red, which may be representative to the Police and Criminal teams. The suitcases do not provide storage nor cash and are merely decorations. As the Badger's appearance and performance are closely similar to those of the Jeep, and the two vehicles are typically compared between each other by players.

name: Banana Car
creator: ZuperPro1 (1392743315)
notes: Rare to find, frequently receives overpays in downgrades
description: The Banana Car is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was the Level 10 grand prize of Season 5: Let the Memes Begin. The Banana Car spawned at the Season Hypezone where all of the seasonal vehicles in seasons under the contract system have spawned. The vehicle also emits a unique banana trail from its back wheels, similar to the trails of the Volt, Volt 4X4 and Concept. The trail is produced even when stationary. The Banana Car has the eighth-highest top speed, beating the Rattler, Beam Hybrid, Volt, Roadster, and the Torero, and is comparable to the Jet Ski and the Stallion. This vehicle also has a powerful launch and acceleration, but the trail left behind lasts a long time, making it difficult to use in getaways. Its handling is usually slippery and off-roading is a little unstable, as it is prone to rolling over especially at high-speed tight turns. However, its high ground clearance makes it a capable off-roader. Overall, the Banana Car is a decent vehicle with a good top speed and launch. One of the vehicle's main drawbacks is its large size, which makes it difficult to drive in smaller areas compared to other vehicles.

name: Bandit
creator: Howsel (93146994)
notes: Rare to find, occasionally gets overpays in downgrades
description: The Bandit was a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as the grand prize in Season 13: Super Villainous. The Bandit could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 13. The Bandit's appearance is based upon the 1960’s Dodge Charger model from the animated film The Bad Guys, having identical designs. The car is an excellent vehicle, having solid stats, an impressive design, a custom engine sound, and being quite fun to drive overall. Interestingly, this car has 3 seats, similarly to the Deja and the JB8. The Bandit is an excellent vehicle, and preforms better than most seasonal vehicles. The car has a top speed similar to the Bloxy, and is slightly faster than the Rattler and Icebreaker. It has a great reverse speed, and is one of the best seasonal vehicles when it comes to launch. The Bandit has great handling, being able to make very sharp turns. The car is similar to the Megalodon in terms of top speed, reverse speed and acceleration, having similar stats. It launches well, pushing it into the 100 MPH range quite quickly, but having slower acceleration past 145 MPH. Its off-roading is above average with high suspension. It struggles to go over bumpy terrain, though it does quite well when going up steep inclines.

name: Beam Hybrid
creator: Badimo
notes: Clean variant is rare to find, duped variant occasionally avoided
description: The Beam Hybrid is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak, released in the 2020 Winter Update. It could have been redeemed for free after reaching Level 10 in Season 1: New Beginnings.  The Beam Hybrid is based on the BMW i8, a hybrid sports car developed by BMW. It was initially introduced by Badimo as a "super exclusive two-tone supercar with working butterfly doors and a high-detail interior". The Beam Hybrid is a futuristic sports car with a striking and dynamic appearance; its design is a perfect blend of sportiness and modernity. Its front features a sleek and closed-off grille, giving the vehicle a distinct and aggressive look. The slim and sharp LED headlights as well as the signature butterfly doors add a touch of sophistication and an overall futuristic vibe to the vehicle. Players had 45 days from the beginning of the season to grind levels towards the Beam Hybrid, which became unobtainable when Season 1: New Beginnings ended. It is stated to be the rarest vehicle in the game by the developers, and is hardly ever seen because not many players own it. In terms of overall performance, the Beam is balanced. It features decent acceleration, handling and surprisingly proficient off-roading. However, the Beam Hybrid is slower than some supercars, including all hypercars. It is, however, faster than most general vehicles.

name: Beignet
creator: RallySubbie (71744786)
description: The Beignet is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as the grand prize in Season 6: Race to the Top. It currently has the seventh-highest top speed in-game. The Beignet was obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 6: Race to the Top. The Beignet is based on the 2020 Bugatti Bolide Concept. It is a two-seater hypercar. The vehicle's appearance is notable for its cross-shaped headlights and sharp edges. The Beignet has two customizable body colors. As the model comes with a pre-equipped spoiler, players cannot customize the spoiler. As Season 6: Race to the Top has ended, the Beignet can now only be obtained through trading. The Beignet can be described as a track focused Eclaire; it has a lesser top speed and acceleration but the launch is superior, with improved handling. This makes the Beignet one of the top-performing vehicles in the game, only beaten by the Proto-08, Power-1, Roadster, Aperture, Successor, Concept and Volt in short distances, and the Torpedo, Brulee and Javelin in long distances. Overall, the Beignet has incredible performance and was a seasonal vehicle worth getting. With good driving skills, this car is among the fastest modes of transport. This makes it the current seventh-fastest vehicle in-game, only after the Torpedo, Brulee, Parisian, Javelin, Concept, and Eclaire.

recent_changes.current_value: Clean variant frequently gets overpays, dupe variant occasionally gets overpays

name: Bloxy
creator: Sharky_Cat109 (146212681)
description: The Bloxy was a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as the grand prize and one of the two vehicle prizes of Season 11: Toys and Collectibles. The Bloxy could be obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It could have been found at the Season Hypezone during Season 11: Toys and Collectibles. Based on the LEGO Speed Champions Ferrari F40, the Bloxy is essentially a LEGO vehicle with Roblox studs and two seats, a quite unique vehicle among the many in Jailbreak. Top speed caps out at 206 MPH. It is slightly faster than the Icebreaker, Sentinel, Monster Truck, and Rattler, but beaten by the Torero. It is fast for a seasonal vehicle, but the slowest among all Ferrari vehicles in game - the Stallion and Concept.

name: Boxer
creator: Badimo
description: The Boxer is a sports car in Jailbreak that was added in the Fall 2020 Update, replacing the retired Posh. It spawns at the Museum parking lot. A special feature of the Boxer is, by pressing the G key on laptop/PC, (or tapping the GUI icon on the top for mobile players) the soft-top can be toggled on or off. This is the second vehicle to have this feature, after the Jeep. The Boxer used to have a mediocre price to performance ratio, however, as of the July 2022 Update, the Boxer has been buffed featuring much better stats than before, able to beat the Model 3, Raptor, SWAT Van and now ties with the Cybertruck. Its only slightly behind the La Matador, Interrogator, Celestial and the Jackrabbit. The Boxer is now a pretty good vehicle for its price. Compared to the retired Posh, the Boxer can be considered superior. It features similar launch, handling, speed and reverse speed abilities than it's predecessor, but surpasses it in terms of acceleration, off-roading and braking while being more cheaper. Overall, the Boxer is a good vehicle, rivaling similarly priced vehicles, like the Badger, Challenger and Patrol. It is recommended to buy this vehicle since it has a fairly good stats and spawns at the Museum, making the Boxer a great getaway vehicle.

name: Brulee
creator: Badimo
description: The Brulee (formerly named Bugatti) is a now-unobtainable hypercar in Jailbreak, added in the Jewelry Store Update of June 4th, 2017. It is the former fastest vehicle in the game, beating even the Torpedo, yet losing to the Scorpion. The Brulee is notable for setting several speed records upon release, its top speed and reverse speed being the most notable. The records have since been broken by the Falcon S, Patrol, Drone, Black Hawk, and the now removed Blade in reverse speed, and the Scorpion in top speed. When the Brulee was retired, along with the Steed and Mighty, it received pinstripes going along the length of the vehicle, which could not be changed or removed, being a mark of its service. However as of September 5th, 2021, and the second "5 Days of Vehicles" update, the pinstripes were removed and replaced by a plaque saying "CLASSIC". This has also happened with other retired vehicles. Prior to its retirement, due to its convenient spawn location and excellent performance, the Brulee was an amazing getaway car after robbing the Jewelry Store, where it spawned at the time. The Brulee has the second highest top speed and fourth highest reverse speed in the game. Its launch and acceleration are good, though outclassed by most hypercars. Its other stats such as handling, off-roading, and braking are average. Similarly to the Torpedo, the Brulee can never truly reach its true top speed due the time needed for acceleration and the curviness of Jailbreak's roads. However, it is still able to reach very high speeds if given enough time. Even to this day, its overall performance is solid when compared to other vehicles.

name: Camaro
creator: Badimo
description: The Camaro is one of the four free starter vehicles in Jailbreak that can be used by any player, with the other three being the Jeep, Cruiser, and Helicopter. The Camaro has been in Jailbreak since before its release. The Camaro's major advantage is its usefulness, as the vehicle spawns in many major locations, such as the Prison, both Criminal Bases, Police Stations, and many heists, making it have the most spawn points out of any vehicle. The Camaro is a mid-low-tier vehicle in terms of performance, being slower than most other vehicles, but is still faster than a selected few. Despite its low speed, the vehicle is still practical due to its solid driving capabilities and easy access for any player, as well as being free. Its off-roading capability is comparable to some general vehicles. The handling is extremely good, making the car quite reliable for transportation. Easy to use and easy to find, the Camaro is a fitting car for beginners. However, the Jeep is a more versatile vehicle to use for newer players than the Camaro, as it has better overall performance and seats two more players, while also being free. As players without the Premium Garage Gamepass can spawn the Jeep anywhere, it is recommended to use it over the Camaro whenever possible. Overall, the Camaro is a decent starter vehicle as it has average performance, and can be found practically everywhere on the map, making it a good escape vehicle as well.

name: Camper
creator: Ickleon37 (847255377)
description: The Camper is the first vehicle added in the 5 Days of Vehicles 2021 event. It was released on August 31st, 2021, and spawns at the vacant lot across from the Oil Rig. Based on the Volkswagen Type 2 (t1), the Camper is the first and only camper van in Jailbreak. It features a driver seat and 5 passenger seats, 2 of which are beds that players appear to be lying down on upon entering the vehicle. This is also the first vehicle in the game to have side mirrors, which, to this day, is still an aesthetic rarely seen on vehicles. The Camper currently spawns at Oil Docks. It used to be located outside the Tomb entrance, but as of the addition of the Oil Rig, it was moved to a parking lot near the Oil Docks. In the 2024 February Update, the parking lot was removed to make way for a pass to the Mountain Lookout that was added alongside the update, and was relocated once again to where it is now. The Camper is a vehicle often compared to the Cybertruck. Its performance is comparable to that of the Model 3 or the Posh, but with more seats. What it lacks in performance, it makes up for in styling.

name: Carbonara
creator: Plasmazillasc (1009227115)
description: The Carbonara is a limited-time vehicle in Jailbreak. It is the first Pagani-inspired vehicle in Jailbreak, and was released on December 22, 2023, it was only available for 5 days, until December 27, 2023. It is primarily based of the Pagani Zonda Cinque Roadster, with design tweaks and modifications based off of various Pagani models. The Carbonara was seen in the Unknown Crate when it was still available for purchase. Customization wise, the Carbonara is one of the most flexible vehicles in Jailbreak. It is the first car with a three-section customizable body, which can all be customized separately from each other. The outermost section falls under the "Body" category within the garage, while the middle section can be configured by the "Second Body" option. The center stripe's color can be customized within the "Interior Detail" section in the Garage. Interior customization is split within the "Interior" and "Seats" category. Lastly, the rear spoiler can be customized and swapped out from the default Carbonara spoiler if desired by the player. The Carbonara has excellent performance for a Jailbreak vehicle, with comparable performance to the Eclaire or Parisian. It has a well-balanced mix of high top speed, stable handling, and good acceleration. The Carbonara has a special performance tune using a five-speed transmission which uses long gear ratios to simulate a realistic shifting feel when accelerating. With its upgraded turning speed, the Carbonara handles better than most supercars around tight corners and at high speeds. Within Jailbreak's map, the Carbonara can easily reach and maintain a speed of over 260 MPH thanks to its well-balanced stats. Braking, however, is not the Carbonara's strong suit- With below-average braking performance for a supercar, the Carbonara takes a few seconds to come to a complete stop from high speeds and can be considered the Achilles' heel of the car. Overall, the Carbonara is a very capable supercar with its high top speed, good handling, and consistent acceleration, and can be recommended for mid to long distance trips across the Jailbreak map.

name: Celestial
creator: Badimo
description: The Celestial is a fast car, but not as fast as a supercar. To use the vehicle or the turret, the player must have the BOSS Gamepass. They can additionally use the turret if they have the SWAT Gamepass which also costs 425 Robux. Before, anybody could use the Turret until an update which restricted its use to owners of the SWAT or BOSS Gamepass. The Celestial can be found in 2 locations; the Rising City Criminal Base and the Volcano Criminal Base. This vehicle is more of a tactical vehicle that allows players to utilize it in police chases or driving combat. If the driver is able to maneuver it properly, they can quickly turn the car around while still moving backward, and give the player on the turret a chance to get a shot at the vehicles in pursuit. While it is rather difficult to judge this vehicle in terms of performance due to it costing Robux, the Celestial is considered slightly above average in performance, with decent acceleration and average top speed and good handling. Just like the relationship between the Torpedo and Arachnid, the JB8 and Raptor or the La Matador and Surus, the Celestial is around the same speed as its Police counterpart, the SWAT Van. A unique feature this vehicle possesses is a minigun on top of it for a third player to utilize, although it is more of a miss than a hit in terms of usefulness as it only deals 2 damage per shot, and has a rather long reload time. In long term, the Celestial is an additional bonus to the Boss Gamepass. While it is recommended to buy this gamepass, the Celestial is not the main reason why they should. Sometimes when off-roading, due to Turret and overall weight of the car, it can tip over and slow you down.

name: Celsior
creator: Sharky_Cat109 (146212681)
description: The Celsior is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. Modeled by SharkyCat109, it was featured as the grand prize of Season 8: Looking to the Future. The Celsior could be obtained by reaching Level 10 through contracts. Unlike previous seasonal vehicles, the Celsior's value is "Free" in the garage. The Celsior appears to be inspired by the Rolls-Royce 103EX concept vehicle, with a heavily modified exterior and interior, plus the ability to hover. The vehicle's most remarkable appearance traits are its arrowhead-shaped front-end, sleek and futuristic design, and scissor doors. Hologram stairs appear by the doors when it is idle. The Celsior has a top speed which has been tested to be within 114-115 MPH after the nerf, sometime after the trailer where it was around 130 MPH. In contrary, its acceleration is among the best of the game. The vehicle's reverse speed only reaches 76 MPH, unlike other aerial vehicles which share the same reverse speed with their forward speed. The Celsior has great maneuverability and fast steering response time, outperforming other aerial vehicles, accompanied by decent braking. Its maximum height altitude is very slightly lower than the Crew Capsule's while on flat ground, but this has little effect due to its hovering physics. However, it can become a problem when trying to go over very tall and steep terrain in a quick period of time. Overall, the Celsior's hovering abilities and its 4 seats may be able to compensate for some of its drawbacks.

name: Challenger
creator: Badimo
description: The Challenger is an American muscle car that was released as the final part of the 5 Days 5 Vehicles Update week. It has 2 seats, and features a detailed interior and first-person driving. The Challenger is the direct replacement to the Steed, a muscle car that was retired in March 2020. The Challenger costs Cash Icon59,000, providing much better performance than its predecessor. The Challenger could be found at the Mountainside Town Gun Shop at the time of its release. The Challenger was later moved to the newly-revamped Power Plant in Part 2 of the 5 Days Of Vehicles update in July 2020, replacing the spawn of the Camaro there. The spawn point was moved yet again, back to the Mountainside Town Gun Shop in 2020 Winter Update, as the Delorean replaced its spawn point at the Power Plant, where it still remains today. The Challenger's good performance, cheap price and close proximity to the Prison make it common among many new players, and is highly suggested by Jailbreak veterans as a newbie's first vehicle of choice. In terms of overall stats, the Challenger is a huge bargain especially compared to its predecessor, the Steed. For its price, it is really fast, beating the La Matador, JB8, Beam Hybrid and Surus. It also has one of the fastest reverse speeds of any vehicle, decent launch and acceleration, off-roading, and brakes adding to its value. However, it has somewhat slippery handling which is especially visible when it is raining, making it slightly difficult to handle. Overall, the Challenger is a great vehicle for beginners, especially for its price-to-performance ratios with its cheap price.

name: Classic
creator: Badimo
description: The Classic is one of the general and convertible vehicles in Jailbreak. It was added in the Museum Update and went off-sale on January 7th, 2022, along with the Airtail; both went on-sale again on January 8th, 2022 only in new servers, due to a data rollback to users who were involved with an infinite money glitch that was patched.[2] It went off-sale again on January 9th, 2022, making this vehicle unobtainable. The Classic used to be found on a pedestal on the right end at the Museum, making a possible option for a getaway vehicle when leaving the robbery, alongside the Boxer. As of its short return, it spawns on the right end at the museum parked. In the June 2021 Update, the Classic also spawned at one of the Tomb exits; its spawn was replaced by the Dune Buggy nearly a month later. The Classic is a fairly decent vehicle for its price, considering its stats. Featuring a powerful launch, acceleration, braking, and absolutely phenomenal handling, the Classic is amazing for using around the Rising City, and its excellent off-roading can make it useful from the Power Plant (only if used with a different route than the optimal one, due to its low top speed). However, it has a slow top speed, losing to even the Camaro despite costing more. Overall, the Classic is arguably better than the Boxer; while the Boxer is faster, the Classic is more agile, a better getaway, and a phenomenal off-roader. If used with optimal routes, mainly ones with off-road areas, the Classic can be a great car to use.

name: Concept
creator: Badimo
notes: Pulls 1.5m in limiteds. Gets overpays in obtainables, otherwise frequently avoided and lowballed.
description: The Concept is a concept car in Jailbreak. It was added on September 4, 2021, as the last vehicle of the Return of 5 Days of Vehicles Update. The vehicle is the most expensive currently in the game and can be found in the Showroom in Rising City, close to the basketball court and the Rising City Bank. Based on the Ferrari Modulo concept, the Concept has a futuristic and unusual design. It features a blue light strip moving up and down the vehicle, spanning the car's entire width as headlights and includes a light bar in the rear as taillights. A unique feature only available on the vehicle is a light strip that travels across the windows from the front to the rear, most likely related to aerodynamics. In addition to the futuristic styling, the Concept includes four seats. The Concept shares some similarities to the Torero. The Concept is one of the fastest vehicles in terms of overall top speed, being faster than most of the vehicles. Acceleration is adequate though the Roadster and Volt do better in a quick escape. Though still being able to easily hit high speeds of up to 270 MPH, the Concept's reverse acceleration and launch is actually superior to its forward acceleration and launch. The Concept's reverse speed is also great, and can hold its own with most hypercars. Both the brakes and launch speed are excellent. The handling is decent, although it can be hard to control the Concept at higher speed properly, and it can be prone to flinging, potholing on the bottom of slopes at high speeds, and popping wheelies while braking due to its design. In summary, the Concept is an excellent vehicle. While the Concept excels in overall performance, it is expensive. However, because it has 4 seats, coupled with its superb performance, it is a reliable vehicle for playing in a squad.

name: Crew Capsule
creator: Badimo
description: The Crew Capsule (formerly named the Crewed Spaceship) is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It could have been redeemed for free after reaching Level 10 in Season 2: Out of This World. The Crew Capsule is also the first aerial limited-time/seasonal vehicle in-game, second being the Celsior. It was available for forty-four days since the season's opening to March 21st, 2021. It is also the first-ever aerial vehicle that can be spawned, excluding the one-off Lia's Spaceship, second being the Celsior. The Crew Capsule hovers off-ground, similar to the Drone and the removed Blade, but reaches a lower height compared to the latter two. It hovers similarly to the Celsior. The vehicle also spins on its axis when a player is controlling it, though this is a purely visual effect. While it affects the player icon on the minimap, you are always facing in one particular direction, regardless of the rotation of the Crew Capsule. Going into first person mode (not via zooming in, but with the keybind/on-screen button) can allow the player to get a better sense of direction, as the camera will always point in the direction the player would actually move in while in first person mode. The vehicle does feature somewhat good launch, handling, and reverse speed. These sub-optimal performance stats are likely because the Crew Capsule can be spawned by owners via the Pro Garage Gamepass and Garages, making it a desirable option for quick getaways. Overall, the Crew Capsule's performance is average, but it can be seen as underwhelming to some. As one of two aerial vehicles spawnable using the Pro Garage Gamepass, it is very often compared to the Celsior, the only other vehicle to share this trait. While almost all of its stats are undeniably worse, even losing in number of seats (3 to 4), it does have some notable advantages.

recent_changes.current_value: Rare to find, frequently avoided and lowballed

name: Crown
creator: JBDelorean (280742494)
notes: Pulls 400k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The Crown is a community-created vehicle in Jailbreak that was added in the Winter 2022 Update, and is the CEO's personal vehicle. The Crown is a vintage luxury roadster based on the 1925 Rolls Royce Phantom. It can be found in front of the Mansion, indicating that it is under the CEO's ownership. The Crown has very fast top speed despite being a vintage roadster. It also features decent handling and reverse speed. It has decent acceleration and launch, but can be outperformed by some lower-priced vehicles like Eclaire and Roadster when it comes to acceleration or launch. Overall, the Crown is a rather powerful vehicle for its price.

recent_changes.current_value: Pulls 666k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
recent_changes.suggested_value: Pulls 400k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds

name: Cruiser
creator: Badimo
description: The Cruiser is an aquatic vehicle added in the Oceans/Ships Update. It costed 35,000 to purchase (prior to the Winter 2023 Update), and features two seats. Like the Jet Ski, the Cruiser leaves a trail of water behind when accelerating. As of the 2023 Winter Update, the Cruiser is now a free vehicle, like the Camaro, Jeep and Helicopter. While the Cruiser may be fast and difficult to control at high speeds, due to its spawn point, it can be somewhat useful as a getaway vehicle if a player finds themselves in trouble at the Power Plant. Due to it becoming free, the Cruiser can also be used by new players as a way to enter the Oil Rig. The Cruiser can turn dark green when it is sailed next to the Bounty Island in a rainstorm. This is an Easter egg shared with the Sloop. Like the Sloop, it can also cause the Bounty Bridge to open up when it is sailed underneath the bridge. The Cruiser is comparable to the Jet Ski and is a better upgrade than it being more controllable, having better reverse speed. Speed-wise, it manages to be overall better than the Sloop and comparable the Jet Ski as it can reach top speed from a standstill in a few seconds. In driving ability, the Cruiser's handling is known to be very sensitive at high speeds. A strong aspect of the Cruiser is its braking, which is better than other water vehicles. The Cruiser is an excellent grinding vehicle, but there is a small problem, people who likes to use arrows are pretty unlucky, they cannot use left/right arrows since the controls are a tiny broken, leaving them using the WASD.

name: CyberTruck
creator: Badimo
description: The Cybertruck is an electric pickup truck in Jailbreak. It was initially confirmed to be coming in the future by a Twitter reply by Badimo, just before the 2019 Winter Update, and was added in the January 2020 Update. The Cybertruck spawns near the Rising City Gun Shop in a small parking lot behind the radio array and next to a building, along with behind the Power Plant. It can hold up to six players; four in the cab and two in the cargo bed, making it a good choice for those who play with many friends. It is the third Tesla-branded vehicle to be added to Jailbreak, with the other 2 being the Model 3 and Roadster. It is often viewed as a better alternative to the Pickup, with the Cybertruck being 91,000 more expensive, as well as one of the most essential vehicles for starters. The Cybertruck is a great vehicle for its price. Its top speed and acceleration can compete with the Megalodon, and it performs impressively in off-roading and brakes. It also handles quite well. However, its reverse speed is sluggish, only competing with the other Tesla vehicles in the game.

name: Deja
creator: Badimo
description: The Deja is a vehicle in Jailbreak that was released in the Balance Update, along with the Ray. This small Japanese car is the first of many new 'affordable' vehicles which asimo3089 has stated he would be focusing on after the release of the now-removed Blade. The Deja spawns in the Prison Police Station parking lot. Prior to the Dream Homes Update, it spawned on a sidewalk next to the buyable house in the town. Prior to the 2023 Prison Update, it previously spawned in the now-removed Prison Garage.When drifting, the Deja features a thick white smoke effect, similar to the one seen in the street-racing manga and anime series named Initial D. Only the Deja, Proto-08 and Parisian had this effect, all other vehicles featured the default thin black smoke when drifting. However, as of the April 2023 update, the Deja no longer has a unique drift effect. Instead, it was replaced with the classic drift effect that most vehicles have. It can be replicated with the “Performance” drift customization option in the Vehicle customization menu. It, the Desert Crawler and Delorean, are also currently the only purchasable vehicles with an overdrive gear. The Deja is also a popular car as of the Town Revamp Update. Prior to this, its spawn location at the Mountainside Town was a factor which made some players not purchase it, as the Town was heavily unvisited. Though, when the Prison Garage was added, Police vehicles such as the Surus, Patrol, and SWAT Van were moved into it, but oddly enough, the Camaro was absent. Instead, the Deja spawned in the final slot, and the Camaro gained a second spawn outside the parking lot. Now, due to its very accessible and visited spawn point and cheap price, it is quite common to see new players driving around in the Deja. Unlike the Mighty, the Deja was slightly slower than the Camaro and Pickup. The acceleration was also not as great, being comparable to that of the Camaro and Pickup. It is, however, cheaper than the Mighty, which somewhat justifies its worse performance. When compared to the Camaro and Pickup, the Deja was beaten in mid-range to long-range travel, but in shorter distances, it was equal to them. Overall, the Deja was considered quite inferior to the Mighty in terms of overall performance and was comparable to the Camaro, Pickup, and the Dirtbike. When using this for fun, due to its unique smoke effect as well as its anime popularity, makes the Deja a very fun car. However, for players starting the game, it was recommended that they purchased the Model 3 for 16,000. However, as of the July 2022 Update, the Deja has been buffed, now having a great top speed. It now has an overdrive gear as well, similar to the Arachnid and Delorean. Its acceleration was buffed as well. It is now able to beat the Camaro, Pickup, Badger, and even the Model 3. It is just behind the Raptor, and a bunch of other vehicles, making it a vehicle worth its cheap price. It also does not require hijacking to get it from the Prison, so it is easy to escape by using the ramp and using Rocket Fuel.

name: Delorean
creator: Badimo
description: The Delorean (known in real-life as the DeLorean DMC-12) is a vehicle in Jailbreak which was released in the Winter 2020 Update. It is found in the Power Plant parking lot, replacing the Challenger's spawn point. The Delorean can glide over water, making it the first land vehicle in Jailbreak that can do so, however, if you dive into the water at a certain angle, then you can manage to go underwater. It was a highly requested vehicle by the community before its reveal. Eventually, it was added alongside the Bank Truck and Season 1: New Beginnings. The Delorean has opening doors and includes 2 seats. It also has a detailed interior to keep in line with the movement to give all Jailbreak vehicles an interior. It makes a good last-resort getaway from the Power Plant, due to its close spawn, all-around good stats, and its ability to drive over water if needed. In terms of overall performance, the Delorean is fairly good all-around. It features good launch speed, comparable to the Model 3. Its top speed beats out most sports cars and general cars, but it is no match for hypercars. Although it is not as quick as most supercars, the Delorean has the ability to drive over water, unlike any other vehicle in the game. It has great off-roading, being able to climb most terrain with relative ease, beating out lots of mid-tier vehicles and comparable to the Torpedo and M12 Molten. As of October 2022, the Delorean now has an overdrive gear, allowing it to reach its top speed even quicker. Overall, the Delorean is an all-around great performer. The hovering ability makes the car a little more worth the price, allowing players to make shortcuts that other land vehicles are unable to make. However, given the current map layout of Jailbreak, the Delorean's ability to hover over water does not give it much of an advantage as most bodies of water have sandy parts on the edges, which allows other land vehicles in water to drive onto land, and the map does not have many opportunities to use shortcuts through water. Despite this, the Delorean is a good getaway vehicle and vehicle in general.

name: Desert Crawler
creator: SpeedQue3n (2463010296)
notes: Pulls 229k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The Desert Crawler is an electric off-roader in Jailbreak that was released on Day 3 of the 2022 5 Days of Vehicles event. It used to spawn near the Military Base in the Cactus Valley map expansion before being moved in the Dream Homes update to spawn right next to the Desert Hideout home. The Desert Crawler has excellent performance overall. Its amazing acceleration is coupled with an over-driving feature that makes it rapidly speed up from 145 MPH to an excellent 231 MPH. It also has great launch, braking and off-roading capabilities, and is one of the best vehicles when it comes to handling. Overall, the performance of Desert Crawler is excellent for its price range, making it recommended vehicle for players with that amount of cash.

name: Dirt Bike
creator: Badimo
description:
The Dirtbike is an off-road bike that was added in the Dirtbike Update. The Dirtbike has one spawn location, near a telephone pole in the Desert/Dunes, and formerly had a spawn at the Train Station, but the spawn was replaced by the Stallion, which was moved there in the Oceans/Ships Update. It seats two people, and can fit into very small spaces easily.
On the positive side, the Dirtbike has incredible handling and excellent off-roading capabilities. It has excellent acceleration and launch speeds, as it reaches top speed quickly. Launch speed can become even faster if the player reverses then immediately drives forward. It also has the almost same reverse speed as its top speed. The Dirtbike's handling is also excellent it can manage sharp turns without steering too much or losing control. On the negative side, top speed is too slow. Slower than free Camaro, Jeep and Model 3. It is used to be cannot use rocket fuel and fly from ramps. As of November 8th, 2023, it can fly from ramps and use rocket fuel. Overall, the Dirtbike is an mediocre vehicle. The Patrol, the police counterpart of the Dirtbike, is a far better alternative, featuring much overall stats while only costing 10,000 more.

name: Dune Buggy
creator: Badimo
description: The Dune Buggy is an off-road vehicle in Jailbreak. Added in the Dune Buggy Update, the vehicle spawned at the Tomb exit near the Water Tower. The Dune Buggy has two passenger seats: one located beside the driver and another mounted in the back. Players sitting in either of these are able to equip and use any weapons or items. The Dune Buggy leaves behind a tire smoke trail, very similar to the trail that is left behind when players press "Shift" while driving. This is most likely the cause of its extreme acceleration and maneuverability. Due to its spawn located by an exit of the Tomb, the Dune Buggy was commonly used as an escape vehicle by criminals exiting the robbery. The Dune Buggy excels in launch and acceleration, competing with the Roadster and Jet Ski in both stats. It also excels in off-roading, better than the Monster Truck. This is due to the fact that it has a light body, enabling it to climb hills with ease. The handling is exceptionally good, being able to do sharp turns easily. However, the vehicle may bounce on low-end devices when players experience lag due to its light mass. Overall, the Dune Buggy is a great vehicle to purchase for its price.

name: Eclaire
creator: Pizzawroles (1469260571)
notes: Pulls 500k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The Eclaire (formerly named the Chiron) is a hypercar in Jailbreak added in the Gun Skins Update. It is known for being an improved Bugatti model that features a detailed interior and sharp design, the first of its kind. It replaces its predecessor, the Brulee, which was retired in the same update. Upon release owners of the Brulee were given 200,000 to help them with purchasing the Eclaire. The Eclaire spawns near the Jewelry Store, which was also the Brulee's spawn point prior to its retirement. On November 8, 2023, the creator of the vehicle announced to his followers that the back of the original model he made was different than the back of the model in the game at the time, and asked if it was better than asimo3089's model. When his followers said that the back side of the original model he made was better, he announced that he would revamp this model again. On December 14, 2023, asimo3089 announced on his Twitter account that the creator of the vehicle had made a new model for Eclaire. This revamp was made on December 15, 2023 with the 2023 Winter Update. It now features more intricate details, a more faithful taillights design, as well as added elements like carbon fibre and a NoobFreak homage on the steering wheel. This makes Eclaire the most revamped vehicle in the game with 2 revamps. The Eclaire has received a very positive reception from the majority of the playerbase. Its top speed is above a large majority of vehicles in Jailbreak like the Airtail, M12 Molten and MCL36, but loses to the Brulee, Torpedo, Scorpion, and Javelin. It has one of the fastest reverse speeds; its launch and acceleration beats many vehicles except for the Roadster, Dune Buggy and Volt; it has good off-roading, and powerful brakes. A previous disadvantage was that it had bad grip, but has since been improved. Its top speed was significantly buffed on November 8th, 2023. Overall, the Eclaire is one of the best vehicles to buy and is one of the best vehicles in performance. Many players recommend it for gameplay.

recent_changes.current_value: Pulls 600k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
recent_changes.suggested_value: Pulls 500k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds

name: Falcon S
creator: Guest5152536177 (975558520)
description: The Falcon S is a superbike in Jailbreak as part of the 2023 5 Days of Vehicles Event. This bike is based off the Suzuki Hayabusa, spawning near the Crater City Bank and inside the Rising City Criminal Base. The Falcon S is a high speed sport bike featuring new rider animations and new physics as part of the Bike Chassis revamp. The Falcon S is based on the Japanese Suzuki Hayabusa; famed for its abundant power, agility, and majestic presence. Legendary for establishing a level of ultimate sport performance and for retaining the number one position for the past two decades in the class it created. The bike has a detailed motorcycle dashboard and two customizable body colors. The 2023 Suzuki Hayabusa reaches a top speed of 312 km/h, compared to its in-game counterpart, reaching a top speed of 290 km/h. The Falcon S offers exceptional speed for its price. It offers amazing launch and acceleration. It is the second fastest bike in the game, only short of the Volt by a small margin, and has the highest reverse speed out of any vehicle in game. However, it does easily bounce from time to time, and due to the mint bike chassis in game, some players may find the steering of this vehicle a bit confusing. Nonetheless, Falcon S is a phenomenal vehicle for its price and is recommended for new players to buy this motorcycle.

name: Fiasco
creator: MrOofXD (367225191)
description: The Fiasco was a safe-exclusive vehicle in Jailbreak based on the Fiat 500. It was only available in Common Safes. Being of Ultra Rare rarity, players have a 5% chance of getting either this or the Cosmic Drift. The Fiasco is a tiny city car fit for a cruise, albeit a bit cramped. It may not run a tire shop, but it is perfect for fans of vintage cars. The vehicle features functional doors. Based on the Fiat Nuova 500, this car is one of the most iconic vehicles in the world with more than 60 years of Italian heritage to its name. It is the successor to the Fiat 500 "Topolino", which was the first car in the Fiat 500 line. The Nuova 500, despite being almost 70 years old, is still a fan favorite among vintage car enthusiasts and Italians the like with many still driven to this day. In Italy, the Nuova 500 is a popular car for tours and rental. To celebrate the 50th year anniversary in 2007, Fiat released a modernized version of the Nuova 500 which is still in production to this day. The Fiasco shares very similar stats to the Mighty. It may not excel in top speed and its reverse speed sticks out like a sore thumb, but its handling and off-roading capabilities are phenomenal for a civilian vehicle. The vehicle's launch, acceleration, and braking are amazing as well, making this a nimble vehicle great for zipping around. The chances of getting the Fiasco in Common Safes is low, however. On average, it would take a player 1 in 40 safes to open (2.5% chance), costing 200,000. It is not recommended for new players to try their luck, and are better off purchasing other vehicles such as the Model 3 or Challenger.

name: Fire Truck
creator: Badimo
description: The Firetruck is a special emergency service vehicle in Jailbreak. Added in the Fire Update, the Firetruck spawns in the Fire Station, located in Crater City. Previously, it spawned at the Bounty Bay Airport and at the old Fire Station, prior to the latter's removal. The vehicle has two unique features: an interactive ladder that can be toggled by anyone for climbing to higher surfaces, and a water cannon that can extinguish fires and push back nearby players. The Firetruck was revamped as part of the 2022 5 Days of Vehicles update with a detailed fireman's ladder, new water effects, and more. The Firetruck's water cannon (enabled by pressing G or the GUI button) can push back enemies and nearby teammates. However, even though the players caught in the water beam cannot escape, they can still use guns to attack the driver, and will eventually be pushed out of range and can escape. This vehicle is fast and can outclass the pre-revamped Stallion in terms of top speed. The Firetruck's water cannon can be used to quickly extinguish fires made by rockets or lightning. Players can do this by pressing G in front of a fire and aiming the water at the fire. This enables the driver to destroy roadblocks made by trolls or the opposing team. However, while using the vehicle, the water cannon cannot be rotated. Putting out fire rewards 100. (120 for VIP Gamepass owners) The Firetruck's ladder can be used to reach the top of some shorter buildings, which allows the player to gain a height advantage. The height advantage can be used to 'snipe' unsuspecting enemies below. The ladder can be toggled by people who do not own the vehicle. This can also damage players on the other team. The Firetruck has a mixed reception. The top speed is very fast for such a heavy vehicle, slightly beating the Megalodon, Cybertruck, and Model 3. Its true top speed can also slightly beat the Surus and the Torero. Its only slightly behind both the Roadster and Challenger. Its handling is rather good as well. However, the Firetruck is plagued with disadvantages. Its launch is among the slowest in the game, the braking offers little stopping power, and its reverse speed is quite slow - though this is expected for a big, heavy vehicle. Overall, the Firetruck has its positives, but its negatives outweigh them. It is not recommended to purchase it as a grinding vehicle, but can be fun to use due to its unique features. Its large size allows it to be an excellent roadblock as well.

name: Frost Crawler
creator: Poogah (2267756092)
description: The Frost Crawler is a seasonal vehicle for Season 19: Northern Winter. This vehicle seats 6 players and has great off-roading, along with a tow hitch. The Frost Crawler is loosely based on the Textron Systems Ripsaw. The Frost Crawler could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 19.

name: Goliath
creator: ShadowSamurai720 (391034465)
notes: Rare to find, frequently pulls overpays in downgrades
description: The Goliath is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as the grand prize of Season 12: Bring in the Military. The Goliath could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 12: Bring in the Military. The Goliath is a military-themed vehicle with a seating capacity of 10 - 11 people if including the turret - which is the second highest within the game. Other notable aspects include a walkable interior and a Minigun at its top. It also has bulletproof armor that blocks players from 40% of weapon damage, similar to the Tank. The Goliath is a vehicle that favors seating capacity the most, being able to seat 11 players. It also has decent off-roading capabilities and great top speed for a heavy vehicle. However, due to the Goliath's large size, its launch, acceleration, and brakes are relatively weak, making it undesirable for getaways and chases. Overall, the Goliath is a suitable vehicle for teams due to its high seating capacity, with launch and brakes traded for that.

name: Hammer Head
creator: MazeGFX (125083697)
description:
The Hammerhead is a safe-exclusive vehicle in Jailbreak. It is obtained from the Police Safes. Being of legendary rarity, players have a 3% chance of getting it. Assuming that the player did not get the Hammerhead throughout the previous rolls, it will take about 1,155,000 within one server sitting. The Hammerhead features a distinct wedge like shape in its front end in which it can flip cars by simply ramming into them. The Hammerhead features many geometric shapes with sharp angles, possibly resembling body armor put on to the car during an apocalypse. The back tires are also distinctly larger and thicker than the front, making it one of the few cars to have this feature in Jailbreak. Thanks to its unique collision exclusive to vehicle interactions, the Hammerhead has the unique ability to act as a ramp. It can drive under static vehicles, making them go on top. Whether the Hammerhead is in motion or not, any vehicles driving head first into its ramp will be launched, all while the Hammerhead itself remains undaunted. This essentially creates a bulldozer effect and allows it to bypass roadblocks with ease.
The Hammerhead features great performance with a high top speed, great launch and acceleration. Additionally, it bears exceptional hill climbing abilities. However, it suffers from poor ground clearance that makes it struggle with more rigid terrain, which can be negated with higher suspension. The Hammerhead also has a unique ability to go under vehicles due to its design, making it very hard for Police to stop, as they cannot roadblock it or pit maneuver it head on. Overall, the Hammerhead's performance is considered one of the best out of all safe-exclusive vehicles, so whether it is worth the gamble and money to obtain this vehicle heavily varies from player to player.

name: Icebreaker
creator: Italimpexfx (173543636)
notes: Clean variant is rare to find, both variants occasionally receive overpays in downgrades
description: The Icebreaker is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak and was featured as the grand prize in Season 7: Ice Cold. The Icebreaker could be obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It could have been found at the Season Hypezone during Season 7: Ice Cold. The Icebreaker is based on the Porsche Taycan. It is described by many players as a "four-seater Roadster" due to their similar appearances, performances, and both being electric-powered vehicles. It is the second vehicle in Jailbreak to have rear mirrors, only after the Camper and before the Snake, Javelin, Power-1 and Longhorn. The Icebreaker has received rather mixed feedback. The vehicle was praised for its great overall performance, as well as its special features, but also received backlash for being especially hard to obtain due to the XP required to level up for Season 7: Ice Cold being increased, despite not having performance as powerful as its predecessor, the Beignet. The Icebreaker's overall performance is fantastic, being comparable to the Roadster, as they feature similar top speeds, acceleration and handling. On top of this, the Icebreaker is also vastly notable for its impressive reverse speed, being comparable to the Airtail. For a seasonal vehicle, performance that compares to these sort of in-game vehicles places the Icebreaker in a great spot.

name: Interrogator
creator: Badimo
description: The Interrogator is a police SUV in Jailbreak that was released in the Season 3 Mid-Season Update. It can be found at the Prison Police Station parking lot and Rising City Police Station. Prior to the 2023 Prison Update, the Interrogator previously spawned in the now-removed Police HQ. In contrast to most Jailbreak cars, the Interrogator is a relatively detailed vehicle model. The Interrogator features a unique side decal, various objects in its interior, such as a computer and Donut, bull bars on the front grille, and lights surrounding the body. As it spawns at police stations, prisoners and criminals must hijack the car before driving it. It replaces the retired SUV. The Interrogator is a decent vehicle. While its acceleration is quite slow, the top speed is faster than that of many sports cars, which allows it to catch up to them in chases. Handling is decent and is controllable with no serious amount of effort, but brakes are somewhat weak. Overall, the Interrogator is a good choice for new police players. It spawns at convenient locations, and is capable enough to catch average criminals, especially with a good Pit Maneuver. But for criminal players, this vehicle is impractical. It needs to be hijacked before driving, and is not suitable for quick getaways. It is generally identical to its predecessor, the SUV, but there are some slight differences, for better or for worse.

name: JB8
creator: Badimo
description: The JB8 (formerly known as R8) is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as the level 30 reward for the Criminal team in Season 3, alongside its Police counterpart, the Raptor. Like many seasonal cars, the JB8 is rarely seen in-game due to its exclusivity.  The JB8 received a name change in a miscellaneous update, from "R8" to "JB8", along with a few other vehicles. This was most likely done to avoid possible future copyright claims. The JB8 has excellent top speed, beating the Surus and Beam Hybrid. The reverse speed is quite decent. The acceleration, handling, and brakes are all good as well. The JB8 isn't great when it comes to off-roading. In addition to its performance, it can hold three passengers, making this a great supercar for trios. Overall, the JB8 proves to be a great car. However, it is outclassed by some cheaper and similar priced supercars in performance.

name: Jackrabbit
creator: oof_ed (551992971)
description: The Jackrabbit is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as the Level 8 prize of Season 9: A New Journey. The Jackrabbit could be obtained by reaching Level 8 through contracts, unlike previous newer seasonal vehicles which could only be obtained by reaching Level 10. It could be found at the Season Hypezone during Season 9: A New Journey.  The Jackrabbit is based off the First Generation Mazda MX-5, but heavily modified for off-roading. As described by Jailbreak's developer team, Badimo, it is "an excellent off-roader with a spare tire, massive headlamp array, and working doors." Attempting to buy the vehicle will give a notification that reads that this vehicle is from another season, as with other seasonal vehicles. Owners of the vehicle can spawn in the Jackrabbit using a Garage or using the Premium Garage gamepass, like any other seasonal vehicle. The Jackrabbit is decent for a seasonal vehicle. It has great off-road performance and a good top speed. However, it is the fourth-slowest seasonal vehicle, only faster than the Crew Capsule, Celsior, and Tiny Toy. Nonetheless, its performance beats those of many mid-tier vehicles.

name: Javelin
creator: oof_ed (551992971)
notes: Clean variant is hard to get without overpaying, duped variant frequently gets overpays
description: The Javelin is a limited-time vehicle in Jailbreak. Released on November 4th, 2022, it was featured as the fifth and final vehicle of the 5 Days of Vehicles Update and was only available to obtain for 48 hours. The Javelin was seen in front of the 1M Dealership when it was still available for purchase. As it has now gone off-sale, the only way to obtain it is through trading. The Javelin has the fourth-highest top speed among land vehicles, and the fifth-highest top speed of all vehicles, only bested by the Torpedo, Brulee, Scorpion, and the Jet Ski.  The Javelin is among the best-performing hypercars in the game. With a phenomenal top speed and reverse speed, quick launch and acceleration, incredible handling and off-roading capabilities, as well as decent brakes, the vehicle outperforms most land vehicles in the game. The Javelin can reach 60 MPH in 0.84 seconds, 120 MPH in 2.82 seconds, 240 MPH in 12.42 seconds and 300 MPH in 21.55 seconds. Moreover, it has a turning speed of 1.54, a 10% increase over most other vehicles, which have turning speeds of 1.4. The Javelin has the ability to climb steep slopes, like the Snake, Beignet and the Torero.

name: Jeep
creator: Badimo
description: The Jeep (also known as the Military Jeep) is a vehicle added in the Prison Update on March 30th, 2019. The Jeep's only spawn is at the Military Base, but it can spawned by using Garages, or the Mobile Garage for owners and non-owners of the Mobile Garage Gamepass. The Jeep is free, like the Cruiser, Helicopter and Camaro. The Jeep is decently fast in comparison to other vehicles in its price range, despite its free tag. It can seat a total of four people with two in the front and two in the back. The Jeep is the first vehicle in Jailbreak with a working convertible top, the second being the Boxer. These perks make the Jeep a more preferred vehicle by new players, being more popular than the Camaro since the Garage V2 Update, due to its overall better stats. The Jeep is seen by many as a better alternative to the Camaro due to the former's higher acceleration and better versatility. It can compete with many paid mid-tier cars when using a Level 5 engine, making it a much more worthwhile vehicle to use. While it is as free as the Camaro, the latter has many more natural spawn points, making the Jeep less accessible, only having one spawn point. Even so, the Jeep is a great vehicle, with high practicality for newcomers.

name: La Matador
creator: Badimo
description: The La Matador is the first supercar in Jailbreak, added in the Supercar Update of April 29, 2017, the second major update following the beta release of Jailbreak. It is one of the most commonly used cars in Jailbreak due to its relatively low price, great stats, and the ability to spawn it for free. It received a model revamp in the March 2021 update, but it's original model returned three years later as a seperate vehicle, serving as the prize for Save the Future community event in OG Jailbreak. The La Matador has received several changes in statistics in-game. In a miscellaneous update in February 2020, La Matador had its top speed and acceleration vastly increased, or buffed, and became one of the fastest vehicles in the game. It was then given a remodel in the March 2021 Mid-Season Update, giving it a refreshed look and a brand-new interior, along with a nerf in performance. La Matador can also be rented for 99 Robux for one day, it is also the only car which can be rented in the entire game. This is not recommended, as it is much cheaper to buy it and keep it forever, than continuously spending Robux on renting it. The La Matador used to have a great price to performance ratio before its nerf. It is faster than the Cybertruck and slightly slower than the Megalodon. It also has a decent reverse speed and launch. Despite being poor in its acceleration to top speed time, La Matador has a fast acceleration rate. Handling on the vehicle is excellent, but it lacks in off-roading. Added to that, the places where the vehicle spawns, such as the Volcano Criminal Base, the Criminal Lair and the Rising City Bank, make the La Matador incredibly useful as a crucial getaway in certain cases. Overall, La Matador is an excellent car, but the Surus could be considered a better option in terms of better speed, off-roading, more seats, while costing only 9,000 more. Still, the vehicle has more spawn points than its SUV brother, and is a stepping stone for newer players into the world of supercars the game has to offer.

name: Laviolette
creator: Zewo_Tsu (102700598)
description: The Laviolette is a safe-exclusive vehicle in Jailbreak based on the 1959 Cadillac Eldorado Biarritz. It is only available in Street Safes. Being of epic rarity, players have a 5% chance of getting either this or the Flame Spitter Wing. It was added on the second day of the 2023 5 Days of Vehicles event. The Laviolette is a sleek space-age muscle car with chrome accents and rear tailfins. The front of the vehicle is dominated by grilles, with four headlights on both sides. The car's surface is almost flat, save for a canopy housing a driver and passenger seat. Its real-life counterpart is the Cadillac Eldorado Biarritz. The Laviolette performs surprisingly well for a classic car. It does not excel in any aspects of performance, but its top speed is only slightly lower than low tier supercars, like the La Matador, and is comparable to the Shell Classic, another safe vehicle that is much more expensive to obtain. The chance of getting the Laviolette in Street Safes is low, however. On average, it would take a player 1 in 40 safes to open (2.5% chance). With that money, players can purchase better performing vehicles, like the Roadster and Eclaire. Similar to the Fiasco, it is not recommended for new players to spend money on safes trying to obtain this vehicle, though it is ultimately a fun and decent car to drive. It is worth noting that the lowriding effect of the Laviolette causes the vehicle's rear to scrape the ground, which can affect many aspects of performance, for better or worse.

name: Lia
creator: Badimo
notes: Most rare and most exclusive vehicle ingame
description: The Lia, commonly known as Lia's Spaceship, is the rarest and most exclusive vehicle in Jailbreak. It was gifted to the richest player in the game at that time, L_iiaa (also known as Lia) after she reached one billion in-game cash. Described as a "custom one-off", this unique vehicle is only available to Lia and is restricted from use to any other player in the Jailbreak main game. As the Lia is only available to L_iiaa, this results in limited knowledge of this vehicle. Many players call it a "Blade reskin" due to their similar functionalities and statistics. The method of spawning the spaceship is through garages, similar to the Crew Capsule and Celsior. This vehicle is technically free, having been added to Lia's vehicle list without any deduction of her cash. When the Trading Island was being tested by Badimo, there was a garage bug where game testers could spawn and drive around in limited vehicles, including the Lia. This was patched soon afterward. The Lia has been showcased to appear in the shape of a fair-sized spaceship with 4 decorative rocket wings and 6 decorative rocket exhausts. Stripes can be seen along its main body and on the rocket wings, matching the body color at any time. In addition, the Lia has a light strip on both sides. The signature "LIA - 1B" can also be seen on its left side. The vehicle has a cabin modeled with two seat rows and a dashboard. Though the cabin is accessible, it only takes a small portion of the vehicle, and the rest of it is seemingly inaccessible. The vehicle has a recognizable smoke effect appearing underneath it, similar to the Blade. Due to the teaser images' angles, many players assume that the Lia has a big size; on the contrary, it is comparable to the Celsior in length, the Volt 4X4 in width, and the Firetruck in height. The Lia has a spotlight, the same kind as those seen on the Helicopter and the Little Bird. It can be shot down like most other aerial vehicles. Like the Blade and Drone, it does not have a rope as the rope is longer than its max altitude. The Lia cannot use textures for customization. The reason for this is unknown. Players who adopted a dog will have their dog sitting on top of the Lia when they drive it. The Lia is proved to have almost identical performance to the now-removed Blade, which can be seen via the game's code. The Lia has a notable top speed. While its top speed is nowhere near the highest ones, its powerful launch and remarkable acceleration let the vehicle reaches the top speed within a short time. Combined with having one of the best reverse speeds in-game, the Lia proves useful in many scenarios. The Lia has great handling and can make fairly sharp turns, but the vehicle can get stuck at buildings easily. Its max altitude is less impressive, being the same as the Drone's, although the altitude changes depending on the ground level. Assuming that the Lia's braking shares the same braking with the Blade, the vehicle would have average braking, taking a small time to come to a complete stop. Despite its great performance and practicality, the Lia is not relevant for gameplay other than for L_iiaa herself, as it cannot be obtained by anyone other than her.

name: LongHorn
creator: EyesOfObl1vion (291914572)
description: The Longhorn is a seasonal vehicle for Season 18: Wild West based on the 1967 Pontiac GTO. This convertible features 4 seats, hood-mounted horns, and a smoothly animated convertible top. The Longhorn could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 18. The Longhorn is a modified muscle car. It features one of the most unique exteriors in the game, with hood-mounted horns, color-changeable wooden paneling, chrome accents, flame-spitting exhausts, and a wide body. The vehicle features an animated convertible roof, the first of its kind in Jailbreak. Its interior is comprised of the same wooden styling featured on the sides, has a non-functioning stick shifter with horseshoe decoration, and can seat four people. The GTO line is often credited with popularizing the muscle car market in the 1960s. The Longhorn has different areas of performance that is comparable to other muscle cars in the game, such as the Challenger and Bandit. With all-round good performance for a season vehicle and and able to seat four players, it is an excellent choice of vehicle if playing with a small group. It may be not as fast as other modern seasonal vehicles, but the Longhorn is perfect for any collector who loves older vehicles or muscle cars.

name: MCL36
creator: Badimo
notes: Was buyable during the 2022 McLaren F1 event for 200k cash
description: The MCL36 was the limited-time vehicle of the McLaren F1 Event, an official collaboration between the McLaren Formula One Team and Jailbreak. It is currently the eighth-fastest vehicle in-game. The MCL36 was located at the McLaren Skyscraper in the Rising City during the McLaren F1 Event. It became unobtainable on March 11, 2022. The vehicle could be bought or driven for free during the event, though players would not keep the vehicle when the event ended if they had not bought the paid version of the MCL36. The MCL36 was added along with the McLaren F1 Event on February 11, 2022, coordinated with the official unveiling of the real-life McLaren MCL36. At the beginning of the event, asimo3089 stated McLaren had not given a final ending date yet, but stated that the event would be most likely to end around March. It was revealed midway through the event that it would be ending at March 10, 2022,[4] but the date was extended to March 11, 2022, a month after the event's beginning. The MCL36 has the tenth-highest top speed in the game. Along with that, the vehicle also has excellent launch and acceleration, and fair braking. The MCL36 has great grip and steering response time, though the vehicle can be hard to be driven in smaller spaces due to its large size. Despite its size, the MCL36 can climb over hills and train tracks with ease. Overall, in addition to its status as a limited-time Formula One vehicle, the MCL36 was a bargain for its price tag. The vehicle's only drawback is its large size, which can be counteracted by getting used to driving it. Since McLaren F1 Event has ended and the MCL36 is not tradeable, its performance is only relevant to owners.

name: Macaron
creator: DydyWasTaken (591295581)
description: The Macaron was a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak featured as the grand prize in Season 10: Rich and Famous. The Macaron could be obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It could be found at the Season Hypezone during Season 10: Rich and Famous. The Macaron is based on the 2019 Bugatti La Voiture Noire. It is a two-seater hypercar. One of the vehicle's notable features is its active spoiler, which automatically goes up when the car moves and goes down when the car stops/reaches above 200+ mph. The Macaron has a fast top speed. Combined with its decently quick launch and acceleration, it can reach high speeds within a short period of time. The Macaron also has great handling ability and off-roading capability, making it an extremely balanced vehicle. The Macaron can be described as an overall better Stallion, only losing to the latter in reverse speed. While it may be the slowest Bugatti-inspired vehicle in terms of top speed and reverse speed, its other stats are generally on par with the Eclaire, like what asimo3089 stated. Overall, despite having a lack of appraise due to its speed in comparison to its siblings, the Eclaire, Brulee, and the Beignet, the Macaron is by no means a slow vehicle.

name: Manta
creator: Stealth_Llama (424239986)
description:
The Manta is a safe-exclusive vehicle in Jailbreak. It is only available in Tier 5/Legendary safes, which cost 100,000. The Manta is a hypercar with a custom design, not being based on any specific real-life automotive brand. According to the creator of the vehicle, its design was inspired by fighter jets. The vehicle has a smooth, aerodynamic design, sporting a fin and even a jet engine at the rear of the vehicle. The windshield opens on a hinge to allow entry into the cockpit, similar to how one would get into a fighter jet, and as such, there are no side doors.
The Manta has excellent performance for a safe-exclusive car, being able to surpass vehicles over twice the value of the safe you acquire it from. It only has 1 gear and high acceleration, allowing it to reach its top speed of 231 mph in a very short time. It does, however, only have standard turn speed of 1.4, so at top speed it can be hard to control. With its great launch and acceleration, it can easily climb most hills and steep terrain, making it great for getting out of a bad situation. Due to its 2% chance to be awarded from Legendary safes, the Manta would cost around 5,000,000 (the cost of 50 Legendary safes) on average, though, due to pity, the average is much lower (as it cannot go above a certain threshold). As such, it is not advised to target this vehicle for its performance, as the average cost to performance ratio is very low in comparison to most other vehicles. In comparison to its predecessor, the Shell Classic, however, it easily beats the price to performance ratio. Regardless, if one is able to able to obtain this vehicle within only a few safes, they will certainly be getting their money's worth.

name: Maverick
creator: Sardaji154 (1420413811)
description: The Maverick is a seasonal vehicle featured as the grand prize for Season 22: Made in America. This sporty truck has a bed, spare, and spoiler support. The Maverick is loosely based on the Dodge Charger SRT Hellcat, incorporating the off-roading capabilities of the Ram 1500 TRX. The Maverick could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It could have been found at the Season Hypezone during Season 22. Some of the Maverick's noticeable features are it's unique headlights, light bar on the top in front of the roof rack, and the roof rack itself which cannot be interacted with. The Maverick has surprisingly good performance for a truck. It has good top speed for a truck, also reverse speed. It launches quickly, however after 150 MPH, the acceleration slows down a lot, making it very difficult to reach top speed. Even though it's a truck, it's very easy to control. The brakes are as good as a hypercar. As expected, the off-road capability of the vehicle is quite strong.

name: Megalodon
creator: Badimo
description: The Megalodon is a retired supercar that was introduced in the 2017 Winter Update, alongside the Volt and the ATV. This car was the seventh most expensive vehicle in the game, and the third most expensive supercar, being costlier than the La Matador, Stallion, and JB8, yet still more affordable in relation to the Brulee and Torpedo, or, near the end of the Megalodon's obtainability, the Eclaire and Roadster. The Megalodon was deemed underwhelming by the community due to its price-to-performance ratio. The Megalodon was retired on July 12, 2020 as part of the Revamped Power Plant Update, along with the SUV and six vehicle textures. After its retirement, attempting to buy this vehicle will now notify the player with the text "Vehicle is retired" In 2021, the Megalodon was given pinstripes to denote its retired status and was renamed to "Megalodon" from "McLaren" later on as part of a wave of vehicle renaming, possibly to avoid copyright. However, the pinstripes marking its retirement were later replaced by a plaque saying "CLASSIC". Its drawbacks are top speed and acceleration, which although fast, are atill quite slow for its price. However, as of the August 2022 Update, the Megalodon received a significant acceleration and reverse speed buffs, with the top speed going from 165 to 198 MPH (later being slightly nerfed to 190 MPH). It got buffed again in October 2023, this time to 200 MPH, though this was a minor change. Everything considered, Megalodon is a great vehicle.

name: Mighty
creator: Badimo
description: The Mighty is a retired vehicle in Jailbreak. Added in the Mini-Cooper Update of May 20th, 2017, it could be purchased with 25,000. The Mighty was later retired in the Gun Skins Update of March 8th, 2020, along with the Steed and Brulee. The vehicle was temporarily obtainable as the Level 6 prize of Season 3: Looking Back.

name: Model 3
creator: Badimo
description: The Model 3 is a four-seater electric vehicle in Jailbreak found at the Badimo Charging Station by the Rising City Bank. Players can also obtain this vehicle in Street Safes which costs 15,000. The Model 3 is one of the first vehicles in the game, added alongside the Camaro and Helicopter in Jailbreaks public BETA release. The Model 3 was revamped and given an interior on July 2, 2020, as part of the 5 Days of Vehicles series of updates.
