Without upgrades, the Model 3 is slower than the free Jeep, however, the car's stats improve exponentially after equipping the level 5 engine (which costs 50,000), almost being as fast as the La Matador. Aside from top speed, the vehicle has great acceleration and brakes, only being weak in off-roading and reverse speed.

name: Molten M12
creator: RallySubbie (71744786)
description: The M12 Molten is a limited-time vehicle in Jailbreak. Based on the Lamborghini Vision Gran Turismo, the vehicle was released in the Town Revamp Update and was available for 10 days. The M12 Molten is notable for being the first-ever community-created item in Jailbreak. The M12 Molten is designed for high performance. It features a futuristic and sleek body designed for aerodynamics, a jet-like cockpit, and a split rear wing along with space for two. Currently, the M12 Molten is the only Vision Gran Turismo car in Jailbreak. The M12 Molten is based on the Lamborghini V12 Vision Gran Turismo. In real life, the Gran Tursimo was a way for Lamborghini designers to go above and beyond. Made specifically for the Gran Turismo series of racing games, the Vision Program is a way for car manufacturers to express their vision for their ideal performance cars. The Lamborghini VGT incorporates many design staples from the Lamborghini brand, such as signature Y and hexagonal shapes and design elements from previous cars such as the Lamborghini Marzal. The vehicle was first revealed in 2019 with a real life example, and made its debut in Gran Turismo 7 in 2022. The M12 Molten has excellent performance stats. It has one of the highest top speeds in the game, extremely quick launch speeds, and powerful acceleration. It has excellent off-roading performance for a hypercar, being able to climb hills and fairly steep terrain with ease. It also has really good handling with turning radius of 1.68, and rarely slips at high speeds. The M12 Molten is overall an excellent and reliable hypercar. It features amazing all-around performance, though it is not the most powerful in each individual category.

name: Monster
creator: Badimo
notes: Pulls 1m when traded for obtainables but only 500k when traded for limiteds
description: The Monster Truck is a vehicle in Jailbreak, and was added in the Monster Truck Update of September 30th, 2017, it was the first 1,000,000 vehicle to be added into the game. As the price suggests, it spawns at the 1M Dealership. It received a model revamp in the July 2022 update, but its original model returned two years later as a separate vehicle, serving as the grand prize for Season 21. Reception for the Monster Truck is negative, as most players favor other 1M vehicles due to their wants for extreme speed. Its launch and acceleration are not great, but the Monster Truck is great at most other things, including off-roading, being able to get over hills and dunes smoothly, as well as go over relatively large bumps and vehicles. Despite the revamp and improved speed in 2022, reception remains negative. With the revamp, the top speed is faster than practical vehicles like the Model 3, Black Hawk, and Patrol. In this area it competes with the Rattler, Bloxy and Torero. Its launch and acceleration still are not the best, but reverse speed is also quicker than before, and also off-roads even better. It can goes over bumps and vehicles easily, but it can't climb hills at low speeds like the Volt. Overall, the Monster Truck is an all-rounded vehicle when considering its stats as well as seating capacity. However, many prefer other 1M vehicles over this, which is subjective.

name: Nascar
creator: Badimo
notes: Was buyable during the 2021 NASCAR Event for 100k cash
description: The #73 NASCAR, also known as the Paid NASCAR, is one of the two limited time vehicles added in the NASCAR Event, an official collaboration between NASCAR and Jailbreak. Sporting a custom livery, the NASCAR was located at the Event Tower in the Rising City during the NASCAR Event. It became unobtainable on November 15, 2021, when the event ended. The NASCAR features a higher top speed than its free counterpart, due to the former's higher price. It is based on the prototype of the seventh generation NASCAR racing car. The only other differences between the two NASCARs, apart from their performances, are their different liveries. The NASCAR can be described as a quicker variant of the La Matador, with better launch, acceleration, and top speed. Overall, the NASCAR is a great vehicle with excellent performance for its price. Its performance competes with those of many supercars and certain hypercars.

name: Nascar 75th
creator: Badimo
description: The #75 NASCAR is a limited-time racecar added in the 2023 NASCAR Event, an official collaboration between NASCAR and Jailbreak. The #75 NASCAR vehicle (along with all other items from this event) was available for only 10 days and is now tradable. Designed to celebrate the 75th year anniversary of NASCAR, it features neon #75 letters that matches headlights, and a wide-body design. The NASCAR 75 was released in Jailbreak as part of an event to celebrate the 75th anniversary of NASCAR. It has a wide body and is built almost exactly like the 2021 NASCARs. It could've been found inside the NASCAR Skyscraper, which was located across the street from the Police HQ in the Rising City. To obtain the NASCAR 75 and keep it forever, players were required to head to the NASCAR Skyscraper and get in the NASCAR 75. They then had to make their way to the NASCAR Racetrack previously located in the Desert, where they would have to drive the vehicle into the blue checkered area. It performs almost exactly like the NASCAR Free, having the same top speed, reverse speed, launch, acceleration, and very similar handling, off-roading and braking. The NASCAR 75 can beat the Camaro in top speed, as well as other lower-tier cars including the Jeep, Mighty, Model 3, Classic and Deja. It has a great reverse speed, also better than the Camaro, and its quick acceleration and launch ability are as well. It handles smoothly, making it easy to use in certain weather conditions, although hill-climbing ability is not quite that impressive. It has amazing braking, similar to the Jeep.

name: Nascar Free
creator: Badimo
notes: Was obtainable during the 2021 NASCAR Event
description: The free variant of the NASCAR, otherwise known as the NASCAR Free in Jailbreak, is one of the two limited-time vehicles added in the 2021 NASCAR Event, an official collaboration between NASCAR and Jailbreak. Sporting a custom livery, the NASCAR Free was located at the Event Tower in the Rising City during the first NASCAR Event. It became unobtainable on November 15th, 2021 when the event ended. The vehicle could be obtained by joining the game during the event. The NASCAR Free features a lower top speed than its paid counterpart due to the latter's higher price. It is based on the prototype of the seventh-generation NASCAR racing car. Due to its free value, the NASCAR Free can be spawned anywhere without requiring the Premium Garage gamepass, unlike the paid variant. The only other differences between the two NASCARs, apart from their performances, are their different liveries. The NASCAR Free sports similar stats to that of the paid version, but with degraded acceleration, top speed, and launch. It performs almost exactly like the NASCAR 75.

name: OG La Matador
creator: Badimo
description: The OG La Matador is a limited vehicle released during the 2024 Live Event. As its name suggests, the vehicle is the pre-revamp La Matador. The OG La Matador was given out to everyone for free when the total cash goal of 2,000,000,000 for the Save The Future event in OG Jailbreak was reached. Players who did not participate in the event were still eligible to obtain the vehicle by gaining cash after the counter hits two billion, even though the event had ended. The OG La Matador is a nearly one-to-one replica of the pre-February 2020 La Matador (then known as the Lamborghini), featuring identical stats and the exact same vehicle model. It is not spawnable without the Pro Garage Gamepass, despite the fact that the vehicle it borrows the original model from costs 100,000, the cut-off point for that attribute. The OG La Matador's performance is representative of how the La Matador was at its release in 2017, having a very high top speed that was designed to be very hard to reach, much like the Brulee and Torpedo. Although it has a significantly higher top speed, the OG La Matador has a much slower acceleration than its present counterpart and takes a while to get ahead of it. For a free vehicle, it performs very well and can compete with the current version and other vehicles such as the Shell Mark-5, but is not that practical due to its acceleration and especially its launch. Overall, this vehicle is generally more of a novelty rather than for practical use, but is definitely a decent starter vehicle.

name: OG Monster
creator: Badimo
description: The OG Monster Truck is a seasonal vehicle featured as the grand prize for Season 21: Rewind. As its name suggests, the vehicle mimics the pre-revamp Monster Truck. The OG Monster Truck could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It could have been found at the Season Hypezone for the duration of Season 21. The OG Monster Truck is heavily based on the pre-revamp Monster Truck, which means it has advantages and disadvantages similar to the original's. However, unlike the Monster Truck, which costs 1,000,000, this vehicle was obtainable for free by reaching Level 10 in Season 21: Rewind. This vehicle is one of the two OG vehicles, together with the OG La Matador. The OG Monster Truck has better off-roading and acceleration, but loses to the revamped Monster Truck in speed, reverse speed and launch. This retro vehicle from 2019 is an overall decent seasonal vehicle, having advantages and disadvantages compared to the present day Monster Truck. The OG Monster Truck has similar, if not identical, stats to the pre-revamp Monster Truck. It is quite good for a seasonal vehicle, as it is essentially a free 1,000,000 vehicle. It has similar strengths and weaknesses to the current version, but is not identical. The OG Monster Truck has a slightly lower top speed and launch than its current version, but its off roading is much better. Due to its massive wheels, the OG Monster Truck very rarely struggles with terrain and obstacles, being able to take shortcuts many other vehicles cannot. Due to its incredibly high suspension, it is also very difficult to PIT maneuver, making it great for criminals. Overall, the OG Monster Truck is a great seasonal vehicle.

name: Parisian
creator: Zewo_Tsu (102700598)
notes: Hard to get without overpaying in downgrades
description: The Parisian, or Parisian EXP-1, is a limited-time vehicle in Jailbreak and is based on the Peugeot 9x8. The Parisian was available only for four days, from February 17th-21st, 2023. Being a quite unique car when compared to other vehicles in Jailbreak, the Parisian features an incredibly loud and powerful engine, as well as amazing acceleration, making it an excellent car for robbing and escaping police. It was released during the Parisian Exp-1 Update during Season 14: For The Champions. It used to be found on a pedestal on the lake next to Cheater Island and near Rising City. This was the same location as where the Torero spawned during its obtainability period from September 3rd 2021 until September 12th 2021. The Parisian has some of the best vehicle stats in the game, featuring an extremely high top speed, great handling, and a very powerful launch and acceleration. However, it has slow braking for a hypercar, and frequently flips over terrain and at high speeds due to its low ground clearance. The Parisian is mainly recognizable for its high top speed and excellent handling. It can beat the Concept in top speed, but loses to the Javelin, Torpedo and Brulee. It has decent reverse speed compared to most cars, though in comparison to other cars at its level, it is not as impressive. It has a powerful launch, being able to accelerate to around to about 80 MPH in a second. It has amazing acceleration, and can reach its top speed quite quickly. It is extremely agile, making it excellent at turning and moving around the map. It is mild at off-roading, but for a track-focused prototype race-car, its off-roading is decent. At low suspension, going over bridges and going down slopes can cause the car to stop or bounce at bumps.

name: Patrol
creator: Badimo
description: The Patrol is a motorbike that was introduced into Jailbreak in the Map Expansion Update. It is one of the few bikes in Jailbreak and costs an affordable 45,000. The Patrol can be seen as an alternative upgraded version of the Dirtbike, offering better overall performance. As of Day 1 of the 2020 5 Days of Vehicles update, the Patrol's second spawn at Rising City Police Stations was replaced by the Surus, which was then replaced by an Interrogator spawn. In the 2023 Prison Update, the Patrol spawn was moved to behind the Visitors Building, at the Security Office parking lot at the Prison. This was it's only spawn.

name: Pickup
creator: Badimo
description: The Pickup is the cheapest regularly purchasable vehicle in Jailbreak. The Pickup can be found in front of the refill stations at the Gas Station. The Pickup was added in the SWAT Van Update of May 6th, 2017, along with the SWAT Van. The Pickup could previously be obtained by buying the Starter Pack Gamepass prior to the gamepass' removal. This vehicle can seat four people: two in the front and two in the cargo bed, making it more suitable for players playing in teams. The Pickup received a revamp on August 7th, 2021, when it was given a detailed interior to fit in with newer vehicle models. The Pickup can be described as a purchasable version of the Camaro due to the similar performance stats between the two, albeit the former has slightly slower acceleration. It is similar to the Camaro in terms of top speed, reaching 131 MPH. However, the Pickup's off-roading performance is slightly worse when compared to the Camaro. All in all, the Pickup is a decent vehicle. With the ability to seat twice as many people as the Camaro, along with its cheap price, it is commonly bought by newer players. Despite so, it is advised to use the Jeep, which is free to drive and has around the same performance, or buy the Model 3, which has much better performance while only costing 7,000 more.

name: Poseidon
creator: ShadowSamurai720 (391034465)
description: The Poseidon is a seasonal vehicle, featured as the grand prize of Season 16: Summer Beach Party. It features a backseat pool along with a premium interior with lighting. The Poseidon could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 16. The Poseidon is an open-top luxury car based on the Mercedes Maybach 6 Cabriolet. Featuring 3 seats, including the backseat pool and luxurious detailed interior. A pool pump can be found in the backseat pool, emitting bubble particles floating up. The Poseidon is a fairly average performing vehicle, which is expected for a large grand tourer. The Poseidon's long gears and rumbly engine sound contribute to a relaxed driving feel which stands as unique among the supercars and racecars of Jailbreak. Unfortunately though, this means that the Poseidon is not necessarily the fastest getaway vehicle- many commonly used supercars can quickly catch up to the Poseidon's top speed, and the relatively large size of the vehicle means that it can become unwieldy in tight situations. Compared to other seasonal vehicles, the Poseidon surpasses the average, just like the Bloxy and Beam Hybrid; though not as remarkable as vehicles like the Beignet. As a whole, the Poseidon is a decent but not outstanding vehicle in performance, as, like the Macaron, the car prioritizes a luxurious experience over sheer speed.

name: Posh
creator: Badimo
description: The Posh (previously named the Porsche) is a retired open-top sports car in Jailbreak. It seats two players, with the player in the passenger seat being able to use weapons while in the car. The Posh was once one of the fastest vehicles in-game and one of the few vehicles to allow passengers to shoot out while driving, but over time it has become slower than many other vehicles in its price range, and its primary selling feature (using items as a passenger) has been added to almost every other vehicle. The Posh used to spawn in the Museum parking lot, making it somewhat practical as a getaway vehicle to go to the Volcano Criminal Base after exiting the robbery. This spawn was then replaced by the Boxer. The Posh's retirement was first mentioned by asimo3089 when he replied to someone complaining about the Drone's price, mentioning the addition of a lower-priced vehicle (later revealed to be the Boxer), and it seemed to coincide with the official retirement of the Posh. The Posh is now unobtainable, but it can be spawned anywhere with the Free Mobile Garage. Not much has changed about the Posh other than receiving pinstripes (which would be later removed) and being renamed to the Posh. Originally, the Posh was a great vehicle. When it was first added, it was faster than most Jailbreak vehicles except for La Matador and Brulee, making it the third fastest vehicle at the time. It had a unique feature that allowed players to shoot while sitting in the passenger seat due to the open roof. As of the July 2022 Update, the vehicle is still fairly good, now being able to beat the Model 3, Jeep and even the Cybertruck. It is also able to tie with the Celestial. However, it is a bit behind the La Matador and Jackrabbit.

name: Power 1
creator: oof_ed (551992971)
description: The Power-1 is a seasonal vehicle, featured as the grand prize of Season 17: Exploring Europe. It features functioning butterfly doors and supercar speed. The Power-1 could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 17. The Power-1 is based on the McLaren P1- a hybrid supercar of British origin. In-game, the Power-1 features one of the most detailed exterior and interior models of any vehicle found in Jailbreak. The Power-1 has a distinctively low and curvy exterior body that contrasts with its carbon fiber underbody. The extensive carbon fiber design extends into the interior, where customizable Alcantara accents surround the cockpit, which includes a customizable gauge cluster and infotainment screen. Additionally, the Power-1 has accurately animated and positioned butterfly doors that open and close upon entering and exiting the vehicle's drivers seat, a custom start-up sound, and a high-revving V8-based custom engine sound specially made for the Power-1. The Power-1's customization potential is expanded with the ability to fit custom spoilers and specifically change interior lighting colors- one of the first vehicles in Jailbreak to have the ability to do so.

name: Prison Bus
creator: Badimo
description: The Prison Bus is a vehicle added in the July 2023 Update. It spawns at the Prison, on the lane that leads to the Security Office. The Prison Bus includes 15 seats which is the most out of any vehicle. The Prison Bus is the first bus in the game. It is a long vehicle with a seating capacity of 15, the highest within the game. Prisoners and criminals need to hijack the Prison Bus, just like any other vehicle that spawns within that confinement, with the exception of the Patrol. Pressing "G" will toggle its front door to open or close. Being a bus with heft, it is unsurprising that the Prison Bus has nothing exceptional in its performance. Its moderately high top speed takes a long time to reach, and braking can be a hassle to some players. However, its reverse speed is above the average, and can offroad surprisingly well for its size. Still, the Prison Bus is more preferable for fun role-playing and live-streaming experiences than to be something with commendable performance, as its main selling points are its unique look and high seating capacity instead.

name: Proto-8
creator: SpeedQue3n (2463010296)
description: The Proto-08 is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as the grand prize of Season 14: For The Champions. The vehicle was obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It could've been found at the Season Hypezone during Season 14: For The Champions. The Proto-08 is based on the Porsche Mission R. It is an electric vehicle, just like the previous seasonal Porsche, the Icebreaker. The Proto-08's performance is phenomenal for a seasonal car. It is well known for having a rivalry with the Roadster, boasting slightly slower acceleration, launch, and top speed than the latter, but far exceeding it's reverse speed and handling. Its acceleration to top speed takes about 10 seconds, which is one of the fastest acceleration times of all land vehicles. The Proto-08's role, much like the Roadster, is to be an extremely agile vehicle in terms of short to mid-range distances. Infact, it's increased turning radius of 1.68 allows it to take sharp turns and catch up to cars far quicker than it, which makes it one of the best to get around robberies in quick succession for Criminals, or hunt down Criminals as Police. However, the Proto-08 pales when it comes to long distances. After a bit of time, it is ultimately rendered helpless against faster vehicles such as the Successor or Eclaire, both of which can exceed its top speed in a very short period of time, which means the Proto-08 has to act quickly to catch criminals in faster acceleration oriented vehicles. It's off-roading, while slightly improved over the Roadster's, is still quite poor compared to other vehicles, as it can catch even the slightest bumps. Overall, the Proto-08 is a really good seasonal vehicle.

name: Raptor
creator: Badimo
notes: Rare to find, frequently gets overpays
description: The Raptor is a seasonal vehicle that was added into Jailbreak in the Season 3 Update. It is based off of the real-life Ford F-150 Raptor and is notable for its off-roading capabilities. The Raptor is one of the two vehicles introduced as the reward for Season 3, the other being the criminal counterpart, the JB8. During its obtainability, it spawned in the Secret Agent Base along with a Camaro. As of now, this is one of the most rarely used vehicles in the game because of its limited obtainability. The Raptor is a vehicle meant specifically for Police, as stated by Badimo, much like the Arachnid from Seasons 1 and 2. The Raptor has good stats overall, but it really excels in the off-road performance. It also has a seating capacity of 6, which is only behind the Black Hawk's 8 seats and the Goliath's 11 seats, making it quite a good vehicle for teams. However, the Cybertruck, which also seats 6 people, and Sentinel's 7 seats and Prison Bus with 15 seats, outperforms the Raptor in most stats until the July Hyper-chrome Update, where the Raptor got buffed to essentially be a superior yet similar vehicle. All in all, the Raptor is a superb vehicle.

recent_changes.suggested_value: Rare to find, occasionally gets overpays

name: Rattler
creator: ShadowSamurai720 (391034465)
notes: Rare to find, frequently avoided and lowballed
description: The Rattler is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was the grand prize of Season 4: Epic Adventures, redeemable for free after reaching level 10 through Contracts. The Rattler can be described as a rat rod with an adventure-themed twist. Its key features include the skull on the front, with glowing eyes for headlights; its gold-plated engine, with eight big exhaust pipes; and its trunk, styled after a treasure chest. The cabin is fairly standard, but the shift lever has a unique touch, that being the golden skull on the tip. In addition to these visuals, the developers pitched in and gave The Rattler various particle and animation effects. When idle, smoke emits from the exhausts and skull nostrils. When accelerating or reversing, this changes to fire, and the engine shakes.

name: Ray
creator: Badimo
description: The Ray is a classic American sports car added in the Balance Update, alongside the Deja. It is located at the Crime Port. Being a sports muscle car from the 1970's, the Ray's performance doesn't quite match up to the supercars of today. However, it still holds up in some regards as being better than a handful of civilian cars, and isn't necessarily a bad purchase. However, there are better performing cars that can be bought for cheaper, and it requires engine upgrades to stay competitive. The Ray's performance is on-par with many civilian vehicles in the game. It accelerates a bit quicker than the Camaro. It has phenomenal handling. The braking and reverse speed are rather good, and the off-roading is average. Overall, while the Ray does not offer anything remarkable in its performance, it is still a good vehicle for its price.

name: Ray 9
creator: ItzTruminator (996508101)
description: The Ray 9 (known in real-life as the 2020 Chevrolet Corvette C8) is the second vehicle added in the 5 Days of Vehicles Update 2021. The Ray 9 is a community creation, and can be found at the Crown Jewel. The Ray 9's performance is a bargain for its price and is usually seen as a direct upgrade to the La Matador. People would usually compare it with the Stallion, another vehicle that costs only 1,000 more with slightly worse acceleration but a higher top speed. The Ray 9 is a very good vehicle and is a solid getaway from the Crown Jewel. Overall, The Ray 9 is a recommended vehicle for new players to buy as a direct upgrade to the La Matador, also featuring better stability and off-roading than its rivals, the Stallion and the Eclaire.

name: Roadster
creator: Badimo
notes: Pulls 600k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The Roadster is an electric hypercar in Jailbreak, released during the 2019 Winter Update. The Roadster is a two-seater car located at the Badimo charging station near the Rising City Bank beside the river, spawning next to the Model 3. The vehicle costs Cash Icon600,000, placing it near the middle of the hypercar price range. The Roadster uses the electric motor noise when driving, which is a quiet, high-pitched whine, similar to the Model 3, Cybertruck, Volt, Icebreaker, and the Volt 4X4. The Roadster gained an interior in the Dogs Update of February 2021. Its exterior model remained unchanged, while the interior was completely revamped. The Roadster is well known for having extremely fast launch and acceleration. Its launch speed beats nearly every other vehicle in the game except for the Volt and Dune Buggy. The acceleration to top speed takes about 7-10 seconds, one of the shortest acceleration times for vehicles in the entire game. The Roadster is one of the quickest cars in short to mid-range distances, due to its launch speed and acceleration, which puts it ahead of every hypercar, regardless of top speed. In addition, its brakes and handling are among the best, making the Roadster an incredible hypercar in many practical cases. However, contrary to popular belief, the Roadster is the slowest hypercar in the game in terms of top speed and slower than some supercars, including the Stallion. It also has a poor reverse speed due to it being an electric vehicle, like the Model 3 and Cybertruck. Because of this, cheaper and faster cars become better options. Overall, the Roadster is a decent vehicle to buy and is widely known as an excellent alternative to the Eclaire for inexperienced drivers due to its superb handling. The low ground clearance can prove to be irritating especially in chases. Even though higher suspension helps a little, it still catches even the slightest bumps.

name: SUV
creator: Badimo
description: The SUV is a retired vehicle that used to spawn in the parking lot in front of the Prison Police Station. The SUV was added in the 1B Visits Update. It was mainly used by Police or Prisoners trying to escape, as players could've used it to jump over the Prison's fences or used a nearby ramp as another escape. It retired in the July 2020 update, along with the Megalodon after the Power Plant revamp update. The SUV sports decent top speed, acceleration, and handling. It excels in off-roading, having high ground clearance and being able to climb hills with ease. Before its retirement, it made a good getaway from the Prison by using the ramp, but this required Rocket Fuel. It makes a good police vehicle due to its great off-roading and reasonable stats. Overall, the SUV is a decent vehicle, especially for teams, similar to the Interrogator.

name: Swat Van
creator: Badimo
description: The SWAT Van is a gamepass vehicle in Jailbreak that can be driven by anyone who owns the SWAT Gamepass and can be acquired at the Prison parking lot. The SWAT Van was revamped on July 23, 2024 as part of the SWAT Toy Event. The SWAT Van can seat a total of up to 8 players: the driver, one passenger next to the driver, three passengers in the middle row, 2 people in the back, and another player who can stand on the roof and use the Minigun, which requires the SWAT and/or BOSS Gamepass. The handling is also very good, and is great for pursuing Criminals. While slow compared to other cars, it can be used as a roadblock so enemy cars cannot pass. Pressing "G" will toggle the rear doors to open or close. At first, only somebody with a keycard could drive it out of the prison, as it is the only place it spawns. But now, there is a ramp in the Prison, so players can drive off the ramp and use rocket fuel to get it out. However, this is still an issue for people that don't have much Rocket Fuel. Players can still spawn a SWAT Van with the Premium Garage gamepass or spawning one in a Garage. In the 2023 Prison Update, the SWAT Van spawn point was moved from the Prison Garage to the Prison parking lot. This was due to the removal of the Prison Garage. The SWAT Van is a decent vehicle. It features decent speed, launch, and acceleration. Other aspects are also decently good, with the handling being the best. This beast also features great off-roading and braking ability. Overall, the SWAT Van is fairly decent and is a good vehicle for police to drive. However, the majority of the players play in the criminal team, which makes it fairly good as well as bad at the same time. As a result, it receives a mixed reception among the community.

name: Scorpion
creator: Plasmazillah (1009227115)
description: The Scorpion is a limited-time vehicle in Jailbreak. The vehicle features rocket thrusters with rocket engine speed, taking the title from Brulee being the fastest car in-game. The Scorpion was on sale for 10 days. The vehicle was found at the Meteor inside Mountainside Town. The Scorpion is one of the most unique vehicles in Jailbreak. Based on the Red Bull X2010, it was built with pure speed in mind. The Scorpion features bodywork designed to slip through the air, with full cowling over the wheels and a smooth glass canopy leading to a flat structure. Functional suspension bars are visible on both the front and rear wheels. Under the massive fixed wing is the car's defining feature, two rocket engines mounted to the rear. This allows the Scorpion to reach unfathomable speeds never before seen in Jailbreak. The Scorpion has some of the best stats of any vehicle, being the new fastest car in the game surpassing every other vehicle. When its overdrive activates after 140 MPH, it obtains blisteringly fast acceleration which makes the car quickly reach speeds upwards of 300 MPH. The Scorpion's speed is the main focus of the vehicle which comes with drawbacks in the braking and handling departments. The Scorpion can quickly become increasingly harder to control as it reaches its fast speeds, especially with its normal turning speed of 1.4. The Scorpion's braking is below average, but is justified due to its speed. With proper driving, the braking can be used to corner well while keeping a good chunk of speed.

name: Semi Truck
creator: Ickleon (847255377)
description: The Semi is a semi-truck released as part of the 2nd NASCAR Event to celebrate NASCAR's 75th anniversary The Semi is not a limited time vehicle, unlike the other vehicle released in this event, the NASCAR 75. It features a custom limited-edition trailer skin received upon completing a quest in the NASCAR event. The Semi is a special vehicle that was added to Jailbreak during the 2023 NASCAR Event. It features an incredibly large body, as well as a long, opened trailer on the back. Inside the trailer, there is a pre-revamped Camaro on the upper part. The Semi is the largest land vehicle in Jailbreak. The vehicle appears to be based on a semi-truck from the Mack Anthem series and has one passenger seat. The rear trailer is opened, suggesting the ability to transport cars. Due to its rear trailer's length, the Semi can be parked across roads or tunnels to form roadblocks. The Semi has very mediocre stats, mainly due to its large size and weight preventing it from performing well in most areas of performance. It has a decent top speed, beating out some other lower-tier vehicles, and a decent reverse speed. However, due the incredibly large body and weak engine, the Semi has lackluster launch and acceleration, and the Semi takes a long time to reach its top speed. The Semi's handling is decent. While the truck itself is strong with making turns, it is difficult to maneuver the entire rear trailer around as it is a separate object from the truck. The Semi is also one of the worst choices for off-roading, as its weak engine means that it has no hill-climbing ability whatsoever. The size of the Semi does have some benefits. In collisions, it can knock back almost every vehicle, unless it is going at a relatively high top speed. If being PIT Maneuvered from the rear, police have much less of a chance of ejecting a player, due to the long walk/drive to get to the front of the vehicle before its tires are restored and the vehicle moves again. Though a vehicle that some players will find fun to play with, the Semi lacks efficiency and convenience for general gameplay.

name: Sentinel
creator: Ickleon (847255377)
notes: Pulls 250k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The Sentinel is a vehicle in Jailbreak. It is the fourth community-created vehicle. The Sentinel released alongside another community-created vehicle, the revamped Volt, in the Season 4 Mid-Season Update. It spawns inside the level 4 base, located beneath the Military Base. The Sentinel appears to be a rugged transporter, boasting angular styling, large wheel wells and tires, and other unconventional styling ques. It can seat seven players, the third highest in the game, beaten by the Goliath and the Prison Bus. It has a very narrow windshield likely takes its inspiration from armored vehicles. There is a commander hatch for the driver to enter and exit through, and 4 seats in the back with screens. In addition, there are two circular seats flanking the hatch. Finally, there are pipes that connect the front to the back. The Sentinel is relatively fast, beating several seasonal vehicles such as the Beam Hybrid, JB8 and Icebreaker, some supercars but it loses to all hypercars. It has good acceleration for a heavy vehicle. It has very good handling capabilities and includes decent braking ability, although it has a rather sluggish launch speed. A major selling point of the vehicle is the amount of seats it has. A major problem however is its terrible spawn location, being near a Garage. Its overall specifications can be treated as a direct upgrade to the Interrogator, another vehicle also intended for squad police usage and while despite the impracticality of having a seating capacity of 7 and a very poor choice for a spawn location, the ratio of decent performance to price makes the Sentinel a cheap alternative to million dollar vehicles such as the Black Hawk.

name: Shell Classic
creator: oof_ed (551992971)
notes: Rare to find, frequently avoided and lowballed
description: The Shell Classic was a safe-exclusive vehicle in Jailbreak. It was only available in Tier 5/Legendary safes, which could be bought for 100,000. Being of Legendary rarity, players had a 3% chance of getting either this, the Tesla Spoiler or the Tesseract Rims. The Shell Classic was removed from Tier 5 safes as part of the Fall 2024 & Season 23 Update with the Legendary safe revamp. The Shell Classic is a vintage roadster. According to the creator, it is based off the Shelby Cobra 427. The vehicle has an open top, stripes running down the car, and side-mounted exhaust pipes. The Shell Classic has good performance, beating out the La Matador in most ways and being on the level of most mid-tier supercars, and its good clearance makes it less likely to fling on roads than other supercars. Due to its 1% chance to be awarded from Legendary safes, the Shell Classic costed around 10,000,000 (the cost of 100 Legendary safes) on average. As such, it was not advised to target this vehicle for its performance, as the average cost to performance ratio is very low in comparison to most other vehicles of its tier at the time.

name: Shell Mark 5
creator: AMiguent (469555177)
description: The Shell Mark-5 is a community-created vehicle added to Jailbreak as part of the 2022 5 Days of Vehicles Event. Located by the Crater City Gun Shop, the Shell Mark-5 vehicle is modeled after the real-world 2020 Ford Mustang Shelby GT500. The Shell Mark-5 is an excellent vehicle. Its top speed can beat many vehicles pricier than it like the Ray 9 and can beat most generic vehicles, like the Challenger, and even a couple supercars, like the La Matador, and previously the Roadster. Its reverse speed is also pretty decent, comparable to multiple high-tier vehicles. Before it was nerfed, the Challenger was seen as the biggest competitor to the Shell Mark-5. Overall, the Shell Mark-5 is a vehicle with great performance, considering its price. While its isolated spawn point would be a limiting factor to some, it was still seen as an upgraded Challenger or La Matador.

name: Shogun
creator: EyesOfObl1vion (291914572)
description: The Shogun is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. Featured as the grand prize of Season 15: Drift to Japan, it was redeemable for free at the Season Hypezone after reaching Level 10 through Contracts. The Shogun has proven itself as one of the most anticipated seasonal grand prizes to date due to the constantly requested Japanese theme of Season 15, the popularity of Mazda cars among the players of Jailbreak, as well as the refreshing features of the vehicle: an exposed engine at the front, exhaust pipes releasing fire particles on both sides and the front (both shared with the Rattler), a one-off custom texture for the car (previously only seen in promotional vehicles) and carbon fiber texturing (shared with the Javelin and the Parisian). The perfect timing of the addition of the Drift customization feature only benefited the popularity of the car. Overall, the beast that is the Shogun is a top-notch Japanese sports car that combines the best details of previously released vehicles, which is the reason it received an overwhelmingly positive reception from the player base. Designed with passion and effort by its talented creator, it was well worth the Season 15 grind. The vehicle is a heavily modified Mazda RX-7. It has been described by the creator as a "menacing, quad-rotor, untamed drifting beast". The Shogun rivals many race cars that are already in the game. While it does not have outstanding performance, it is still one of the best among all season vehicles. It also has an overdrive gear, a feature only shared with a few vehicles. Its acceleration increases rapidly once it reaches 140 MPH.

name: Sloop
creator: Badimo
description: The Sloop is one of the 1M vehicles in Jailbreak, and the only watercraft to cost 1M. It was added in the Ocean/Ships 3 Years Update on April 4, 2020. The Sloop is located in the Pirate Hideaway, behind a waterfall east of the red suspension bridge, the Bounty Bay Airport and the Power Plant. It is far away from most major map locations. Players can climb up to the crow's nest on the Sloop by the shroud leading to it. The Sloop will transform into a ghost ship if sailed near the sunken ship at the Bounty Island during a rainstorm. This is an easter egg that was added in the Nukes Update. The ghost variant of the Sloop is made of green wood and tattered sails. After the ship transforms, a pirate sound effect and an audio file from the Spongebob SquarePants episode Shanghaied featuring the Flying Dutchman will play.  The Sloop has the slowest top speed, the second worst reverse speed in-game, the worst being Jet Ski, worst launch speed and horrible acceleration. The handling of the ship is also easy to use and handles best in an open, large body of water. Overall, the Sloop is not meant to be for performance, and instead is simply a vehicle meant for fun and collecting. If one wants a vehicle for grinding and practical purposes, the Sloop should be at the bottom of their list.

name: Snake
creator: oof_ed (551992971)
description: The Snake is a limited-time vehicle in Jailbreak and was only available for 11 days, from July 1-11, 2022. The Snake is based off the McLaren Senna. One of the vehicle's prominent features is its animated rear spoiler, which tilts up while slowing down. Another standout point of the vehicle are its side mirrors (something only two other vehicles have), and its very detailed interior, having an accurate screen, a unique steering wheel, and climate control vents, just like the Javelin. The Snake could be seen on a pedestal in front of the 1M Dealership while it was available. According to asimo3089, it was supposed to be available for only 7 days. As it has gone off-sale, the only way to obtain it is via trading. Owners of the Snake could not trade it away until it went off-sale. The Snake delivers high-end performance, which is expected from its price tag. While the vehicle's top speed is not as high as the Airtail, the McLaren brethren,  it is by no means slow, reaching a top speed of 240 MPH. Combined with its powerful launch, its handling is more responsive and the off-roading is slightly better than the Airtail, which balances this tradeoff. Compared to other hypercars, the Snake has phenomenal handling. Like the MCL36, the Snake has a sharp turning radius compared to other vehicles. Another remarkable feature is its off-roading, which is among the best of all supercars. It can drive up mountains and has no trouble clearing some of the steepest terrain on the Jailbreak map. Overall, the Snake can be best summed up as having a moderate supercar's top speed paired with its top-tier handling; otherwise, an Airtail merged with a Roadster.

name: Speed Yacht
creator: Badimo
notes: Pulls 250k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The Speed Yacht is an aquatic vehicle added in the 2023 Winter Update.  It spawns at the Oil Rig. This boat has 6 seats and is among the fastest boats in the game. It can be found at the Oil Rig, and at the dock across from the Oil Rig, as this robbery features being in the ocean. It can also be used as an escape method from the Oil Rig and is one of the 4 water vehicles in Jailbreak.

name: Stallion
creator: Badimo
description: The Stallion (originally the Ferrari before Miscellaneous Update 103) is a supercar vehicle in Jailbreak. It was released in the Monster Truck Update, alongside the Steed and Monster Truck. The Stallion is widely known in the community as a rival to the La Matador due to the constant comparison of performance between the two vehicles. The Stallion has gone through many changes over time, the most notable of which is when it received a complete remodel on July 3rd, 2020, as part of the 2020 5 Days of Vehicles Update, as well as a major performance buff that made it vastly superior to its previous version. The new and improved Stallion features a detailed and customizable interior, first-person driving, and a visible engine. However, its model lacks the exhaust pipes the old model had. This is a similar case to the Surus and Challenger, which were added in the same week the Stallion was revamped. Upon release, the Stallion was a great vehicle. The Stallion featured better overall stats than the La Matador, making it better in terms of practicality. In the 5 Days of Vehicles Update, the Stallion was revamped. It received an interior along with a significant buff. It was now able to compete with the La Matador, and so it gained consideration as one of the best vehicles. Very little has changed about it ever since, other than a name change in a miscellaneous update, where it was renamed the Stallion. This was most likely done to avoid copyright issues. In the Return of the 5 Days of Vehicles Update, the Stallion gained its original place when the La Matador was accidentally slowed down. After conversations with players, Badimo did not return the La Matador to its former speed, but rather buffed it to be slightly faster. Due to the fall of the La Matador and the obscure stats of the Ray 9, the Stallion came to be the best-performing low-priced supercar. In the Private Jet Update, the Stallion received an unexpected buff and slight model changes, along with 2 new spawns. The Stallion spawned at Bounty Bay Airport and Crater City Airport, alongside its existing spawn location at the Town Station. As for cosmetic changes, its grill mesh color was changed. Since its revamp in July 2020, the Stallion is now a vehicle with incredible stats all-around. Its top speed is the fifth fastest out of all purchasable cars in the game, topping at 250 MPH, and its reverse speed is among the best of all supercars and hypercars, reaching 74 MPH. Its launch and acceleration are quick, as well. It also has great handling capability. However, the Stallion has a low ride height. This can prove to be a problem on train tracks. Still, with raised suspension, its off-roading capability is comparable to the Eclaire with normal suspension. Prior to that, the Stallion was a lesser-performing La Matador, a vehicle half its price. Due to the La Matador's unintentional buff, the Stallion was outclassed by it in almost every way. Its launch was powerful, but weak when compared to other similar vehicles at the time. After the buff of the Surus and La Matador, both featured far better top speed, acceleration and launch than the Stallion. The JB8 was also better as it had better stats despite its similar price. The Stallion was a well-performing car, but it was overpriced and outclassed by many cheaper alternatives. Overall, the Stallion is an incredible car for its price, boasting high-tier performance for a great price.

recent_changes.current_value: Pulls 200k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds

name: Steed
creator: Badimo
description: The Steed (formerly named the Mustang before Miscellaneous Update 103) is a now-retired classic muscle car in Jailbreak. It came out alongside the Stallion and Monster Truck in the Monster Truck Update. The Steed was retired in the March 2020 Update, along with the Mighty and Brulee. When the Steed was retired, it received pinstripes going along the length of the vehicle, which could not be changed or removed, being a mark of its service.  The Steed got a replacement in the "5 Days 5 Vehicles" update which was revealed to be the Challenger, a muscle car that spawns at Mountainside Town Gun Shop until the Power Plant was revamped, and the Challenger was moved there. However, it was moved back to the Gun Shop, proving that it was the replacement of the Steed. The Steed in terms of performance was rather mixed, having the best and worst of most cars, featuring quick launch and great braking, alongside a convenient spawn location, but the Steed had a few downsides. Among some of the things that made the Steed deemed a rather poor option to purchase were how it reacted to low-end devices/laggy internet, and its top speed and rather solid but still less than average acceleration. Like many cheaper cars, its top speed was below that of the Model 3, and the Steed's reaction to low-end devices/laggy internet was something not normal at the time. If the device the player is on has an FPS level of below 20, the Steed will start to rapidly bounce up and down, making it nearly impossible to drive. These factors drove players away from the car, despite being among one of the first cars players would see upon heading into the Mountainside Town as it spawned at the Mountainside Town Garage (original). It was retired in March 2020. While the Challenger is objectively a better vehicle, the Mustang's amazing handling allows it to handle tight corners better than the Challenger. Additionally, new affordable vehicles, such as the Ray, are slower than the Steed, which was once deemed slow. As for the Steed's speed, it is about the same as the Jeep. Overall, the Steed was a satisfactory vehicle for its price. It's handling and braking are amazing, and allow the Steed to be useful in many scenarios, though its low speed, poor acceleration and frequent bouncing on laggy devices/internet drove players away.

name: Striker
creator: MrYomelS (1045693193)
description: The Striker is a seasonal vehicle featured as the grand prize for Season 20: Apocalypse. It can seat 3 people, features a custom skin and has a minigun mounted on top. The Striker could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It was found at the Season Hypezone during Season 20. The Striker has a mostly custom design, borrowing little elements from the Nissan GT-R R35. It features sharp front ploughs, a jerrycan to the left, a stop sign on its rear and spikes adorned on several parts of the vehicle. Its custom texture features text, reading "HELP!" on one side and "HELLO?" on the other. Additionally, like the Celestial, SWAT Van, and Goliath, it is mounted with a Minigun on the top that can be used by passengers. The Striker features mediocre performance for a seasonal car. Its top speed is 168 MPH, which is behind every seasonal grand prize but the Frost Crawler, but with notably weaker acceleration. One great aspect of its performance is its durability, Due to it being an armoured vehicle, the Striker has more vehicle health than most and can shield the driver and passengers from 20% of weapon damages. Overall, the Striker is an average vehicle performance-wise, at least in comparison to other seasonal vehicles. Players often view this as a seasonal version of the Celestial, as it performs similarly.

name: Successor
creator: ItzTruminator (996508101)
notes: Pulls 500k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The Successor is a vehicle in Jailbreak based on the second-generation Ford GT. It can be found at the Mansion. It was added on the third day of the 2023 5 Days of Vehicles event. The Successor features a typical supercar design, bearing a sleek visage, and a common stripe down the centre. The Successor was built to carry on a legacy. The Successor features a sleek and aerodynamically efficient design inspired from some of the best Le Mans racing cars, along with a carbon fiber underbody. The front of the Successor features a unique split headlight design, along with nose and vent elements inspired by its predecessor, the first-generation Ford GT. The rear of the Successor features flying buttresses that help redirect more air to the rear wing. The wing of this car features active aero that adapts to how much downforce is needed for the best performance while driving. The Successor has an intricate interior, featuring a detailed digital instrument display, center touchscreen, center console, air vents, and a floating dash. The Successor is among the best performing cars in the game. Its top speed may be short of cars like the Eclaire, but the Successor sports incredible acceleration and launch surpassing many vehicles. This grants it very strong climbing abilities, which can help it traverse off-road with ease, and scale mountains or other steeper slopes. No matter the vehicle's speed, the Successor is easy to handle. Overall, the Successor is a phenomenal vehicle and is recommended for players to buy at some point during their progression.

recent_changes.current_value: Pulls 649k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
recent_changes.suggested_value: Pulls 500k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds

name: Surus
creator: Badimo
description: The Surus is a performance SUV in Jailbreak and the first of the 5 Vehicles in the 5 Days 5 Vehicles Update occurring between June 30, 2020, and July 4, 2020. It replaced the now retired SUV. The Surus previously spawned inside the Prison Garage prior to the destruction and revamp of the Prison during the 2023 Prison Update. It now spawned near the Modern Town Home in Mountainside Town. The Surus, among other vehicles, was retired in the 2026 Live Event & Update. The Surus originally spawned at the Prison's parking lot, similar to the original SUV, prior to the addition of the Prison Garage. The vehicle also had a spawn location at the Rising City Police Station, but the spawn was removed when the Interrogator was released. Now spawning near the Modern Town Home in Mountainside Town. The Surus' great performance, fairly cheap price, and four seats combine to make an excellent team vehicle. However, due to its only spawn at the Prison Garage, Prisoners and Criminals had to hijack the vehicle before driving it, making the Surus a less popular vehicle. This has been changed since the 2023 Prison Update as its spawn has been moved. The Surus has great performance. Its top speed is really good for it's price, comparable to the Icebreaker and far faster than it's fellow Lamborghini rival, La Matador. However, it is far behind the Stallion. Its launch and acceleration are powerful, nearly comparable to the La Matador. Its off-roading ability is also great, being able to climb hills and terrain without losing much momentum. Overall, the Surus is an excellent vehicle. It is suggested that players purchase the Surus for playing in teams or as Police, as it has multiple seats and good performance.

name: Tank
creator: Badimo
description: The Tank is a vehicle that was added in the Winter 2021 Update, on December 18, 2021 and spawns in the Military Base. The Tank was revamped as part of the be revamped as part of the The Epic Bug Fix Update released on October 3rd, 2025. The Tank is known for having a powerful main gun, great off-roading, and having slow speed. The Tank has the ability to fire shells while being driven, with each set of ten shells costing 5,000. It deals massive splash damage per shot, and a maximum of 65 damage within a certain radius. It also has protective armor, which reduced damage taken for anyone inside by 60%. These characteristics paired with its strong control over movement make it great weapon for fighting multiple enemies. The turret can rotate a full 360°, it also has great elevation and depression angles. If the Tank has been shot too much, it will be temporarily disabled rather than having its treads destroyed. Black smoke will fume out of the vehicle, similar to what happens with helicopters and the UFO when they are disabled, minus the beeping sound. The Tank can be towed by aerial vehicles, despite its large size. If a player is sitting in the seat on the right of the drivers seat, they will gain control over the turret removing control from the driver. The Tank has a below average top speed, slower than that of the Camaro. Its reverse speed is around half of its forwards top speed. Despite this, the Tank's excellent launch and acceleration makes up for that. Additionally, its braking is almost instant. The vehicle can be hard to control around narrower roads and sharp corners due to its enormous size and almost instantaneous turning capabilities. The Tank has notably great off-roading performance, climbing over moderately steep hills, players, and other objects with ease. Overall, the Tank is not recommended as a general use vehicle due to its unexceptional top speed and large size.

name: Tiny Toy
creator: Badimo
description: The Tiny Toy is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. It was featured as one of the two vehicle prizes for Season 11: Toys and Collectibles. The Tiny Toy could be obtained by reaching Level 8 through Contracts during Season 11: Toys and Collectibles. The Tiny Toy takes the regular Camaro and turns it into a Power Wheels car. It is a small one-seat convertible with a big steering wheel. The Tiny Toy might be based on the small Camaro from the 2022 April Fools Update. The Tiny Toy has a similar performance to cars like Mighty. It has notably good braking, stopping in an instant. The Tiny Toy is surprisingly versatile, but it gets held back by its low top speed and single seat, making it unsuitable for groups. The vehicle is fantastic within a short distance, rivaling a lot of supercars, and is likely one of the most suitable vehicles to be driven in the cities as it can maneuver its turns effortlessly. It is also the only vehicle of its size that can pit maneuver, making it surprisingly useful for cops as they can squeeze through the Volcano Criminal Base's gate and catch criminals inside. This vehicle is also useful as a. criminal as it has a very short pit time of just 2 seconds. Overall, the Tiny Toy is mid-tier in terms of performance. While ideal for getaways, the vehicle is not too efficient for grinding on a day-to-day basis

name: Torero
creator: Badimo
description: The Torero is one of the limited-time vehicles in Jailbreak. Added on September 3rd, 2021, it is the fourth vehicle added in the 5 Days of Vehicles Update 2021. Unlike other vehicles introduced in the update, it was available only for a limited time of nine days and became unobtainable on September 12th, 2021. Based off the 1980s Lamborghini Countach, the Torero spawned on a platform placed on the River, located near the Cheater Island. A light beam was placed on the platform so players could find its spawn from anywhere on the map. The platform was removed on the date of the vehicle's retirement. Overall, the Torero is a great limited-time vehicle. Despite being based on a vintage supercar from the 1970s, the Torero has impressive performance that outpaces some of Jailbreak's modern supercars. The Torero's powerful launch and rapid acceleration complement a high top speed. It has capable brakes and handling, and additionally, the vehicle has a notably fast reverse speed and top-notch off-road capabilities.

name: Torpedo
creator: Badimo
description: The Torpedo is a seasonal vehicle in Jailbreak. Added in the 2018 Winter Update, it has the fourth-highest top speed in-game, beaten only by Scorpion, Brulee and the Jet Ski. The Torpedo required either Level 30 in OG Season 1 or Level 5 in OG Season 2, both on the Criminal team, for players to obtain it. The Torpedo has some of the best stats in Jailbreak. It has the third-best top speed, fast acceleration rate, powerful launch, great off-roading, and the seventh-best reverse speed. The Torpedo never reaches its true top speed due to its long acceleration time and the shortness and curves of Jailbreak's roads. However, when the vehicle gets closer to its top speed, it manages to stay ahead of almost all high-end vehicles. Driving abilities are well-rounded, with good handling and braking, and excellent off-roading for a hypercar. The Torpedo is also very reliable, with no tendency to fling or hit railroads. Formerly, the Torpedo was different, performing similarly to its police counterpart, the Arachnid. However, being the criminal counterpart of the two cars, the Torpedo would need to be faster than the Arachnid, thus the car was buffed to have a higher top speed. The Torpedo mainly rivals the Javelin, with both having near identical stats, yet the one with the best stats varies by category. All things considered, the Torpedo is an excellent vehicle, with all of its performance statistics being great.

recent_changes.current_value: Clean variant is rare to find and occasionally gets overpays
Clean variant is rare to find and frequently gets overpays, duped variant occasionally gets overpays
recent_changes.suggested_value: Clean variant is rare to find and occasionally gets overpays

name: Tow Truck
creator: Badimo
description: The Tow Truck is a vehicle released in the March 2022 Update of March 4th, 2022. Seating only two, it spawns at the Beach Town. Teased on March 3rd, 2022, the Tow Truck was added to the game due to being one of the biggest requests given by players.[2] It has the special ability of towing almost every vehicle in the game with its tow hook, even when the vehicle is being driven by other players. The Tow Truck appears to be inspired by the Pickup, as both vehicles share similar shape and design. The Tow Truck's most notable appearance traits are its front bumper and lights, its sleek and modern design, and low rear. It has a towing rope that is always equipped at the back. The Tow Truck's most prominent feature is that it can tow away any vehicle in the game using its hook, similar to how towing by using helicopters works. However, the Tow Truck can also tow vehicles when it is being towed, tow a car driven, and tow vehicles that cannot be towed by using helicopters (e.g., the Black Hawk and all other helicopters). Towing another vehicle slows the vehicle down due to the bonus weight. Additionally, the Tow Truck can also drag the Cargo Ship's crates, which was a bug but intentionally left in by the developers. However, players will not get any money from the crates. A driver whose vehicle are being towed by a Tow Truck can pop the truck's tires to unhook their vehicle. The time needed to hook onto a vehicle with somebody inside was also increased in a later update as its previous towing ability was considered overpowered. The Tow Truck has an acceptable top speed and reverse speed. However, its launch and acceleration are unexpectedly exceptional considering the vehicle type. In addition, the Tow Truck has great handling and off-roading performance, along with fair breaking, making it a vehicle easy to control. While towing another vehicle, its stats plummet significantly, the portion of which depends on the vehicle it is towing. Overall, the Tow Truck is a decent vehicle for its price. Despite so, most players purchase it for its towing ability rather than for its performance.

name: Trailblazer
creator: SharkyCat109 (146212681)
description: The Trailblazer is one of the 1M vehicles in Jailbreak. It is the third vehicle released in the Return of 5 Days of Vehicles Update. It can be found at the 1M Dealership, located in the third slot, next to the Drone and Volt. The Trailblazer can be described as a supercar but modified for off-road use, practically making it a 2 in 1 car. It features a body most likely taking inspiration from an exotic supercar, then removing large parts of the body to fit off-road accessories. It also features a detailed interior. A unique feature of this vehicle is that there are no doors at all, similar to the Dune Buggy. It excels in most vehicle stats and beats the Monster Truck in all of them, (except in suspension) despite both vehicles having the exact same price. The top speed is the fastest of all the 1M vehicles, even faster than the Volt, Monster Truck, Black Hawk, Drone and Jet. It can even tie with vehicles such as the Airtail, Volt 4X4, and M12 Molten. Due to this, it has a good price-to-performance ratio and makes it worth it to purchase.

name: Venom
creator: phindr (76393113)
description: The Venom is a safe-exclusive vehicle in Jailbreak. It is only available in Crime Safes. Being of legendary rarity, players only have a 4% chance of getting it.

name: Volt
creator: TommyIsDaBoss (405577649)
notes: Pulls 1m when traded for obtainables but only 500k when traded for limiteds
description: The Volt is a futuristic fictional vehicle in Jailbreak, inspired by Tron and also referencing asimo3089 and badcc's previous game: Volt. It emits a trail of light while driving, and was very well-known for being a buggy and hard to control vehicle. After the revamp, however, it became less buggy, though at the cost of a decrease in its usage. The Volt was originally introduced in the 2017 Winter Update, but has undergone multiple changes since. Most notably, the Volt was given a complete overhaul in the July 2021 Mid-Season Update. This overhaul replaced the model with a community-created design, improved its driving smoothness, and patched many notable game-breaking bugs (camera-steering and driving over mountains). After the revamp, the Volt is arguably harder to control, especially off-roading due to camera-steering being fixed. It is now more of a road-based vehicle, and is less commonly seen being used. Still, it is incredibly fast. Overall, the Volt is a high-risk, high-reward that is a useful compact escape vehicle. While it is less versatile than its pre-revamped variant, it can still climb hills with relative ease. It is recommended to lower your speed when turning due to how hard it is to steer. While its off-road capability has been reduced dramatically, it is now among one of the quickest vehicles on the road, with much better top speed, acceleration, and stability over curbs due to its new chassis. Overall, the Volt is a good vehicle for both new and old players considering it's price tag and stats. It is recommended for new players as their first 1M vehicle.

name: Volt 4x4
creator: Badimo
description: The Volt 4x4 is a seasonal vehicle. It was the grand prize for Season 3: Looking Back in Jailbreak. Like all newer seasonal vehicles, the Volt 4x4 was free to redeem at level 10. It could have also been obtained for 499 Robux between May 25th, 2021 and May 30th, 2021. The Volt 4x4 appears as a futuristic Volt-styled off-roader, with four massive wheels, a giant rear wing, and dual trails that emit from the rear wheels when driving. The vehicle is as long as the Arachnid, but nearly twice as wide, making it the widest land vehicle in Jailbreak. The Volt 4x4 boasts impressive performance statistics, especially when off-roading. The vehicle can traverse tough terrain with ease and climb up steep hills quickly due to its powerful launch speed. It is more capable and stable than other off-roaders, such as the Raptor, Cybertruck, and even the Monster Truck. In addition to this, the Volt 4x4 is pretty fast, beating out all of the aforementioned trucks, the Arachnid, Airtail, Roadster, Volt, and the Stallion at its true top speed. A disadvantage of the Volt 4x4 is that it has slow braking. On May 2, 2025, the Volt 4x4 received the biggest buff a vehicle has ever received in the history of Jailbreak. Its top speed was increased by 95 MPH and reversing speed by 100 MPH, making it extremely fast for an off-road vehicle. Overall, the Volt 4x4 is a very unique and well-performing seasonal vehicle, with various capabilities that other cars cannot offer. It is also a good vehicle for police, like the Arachnid, with great off-roading abilities and roadblock potential.

name: Wedge
creator: D4EAMER (339428324)
description: The Wedge is a seasonal vehicle featured as the grand prize for Season 23: To the 80's. This angular sports car features animated pop-up headlights, doors, and excellent styling. The Wedge could have been obtained by reaching Level 10 through Contracts. It could have been found at the Season Hypezone during Season 23. Appropriately named, the Wedge is a vehicle that harnesses the best of many classic vehicles while having its own unique twist. The Wedge has several confirmed vehicles that it pulls design cues from, such as the Vector W8, the Ferrari F40, and the Toyota Sera in its doors. The vehicle also takes hints from the Lamborghini Diablo and the Ferrari Testarossa. The Wedge also features an engine visible through a glass engine bay cover and through vents at the rear. The Wedge skillfully combines all of these inspirations into a vehicle that is the ultimate embodiment of the 80s. The Wedge has incredible performance for a seasonal vehicle. It has amazing top speed, making it second fastest seasonal vehicle in contract-based seasons, and third fastest seasonal overall, only slower than the Beignet and Torpedo. Also it has very good acceleration and good reverse speed. It has fantastic off-road ability for a super/hypercar. It has a good handling, which is comparable to the many vehicles. And it has below-average durability, which is comparable to the many super/hypercars.

name: XRK
creator: D4EAMER (339428324)
notes: Pulls 299k value when traded for obtainables but less when traded for limiteds
description: The XRK is a community-created vehicle added to Jailbreak as part of the Season 20: Apocalypse update. It is located at the Mountain Lookout. The XRK is based on a Nissan GT-R and features right hand drive, custom engine sounds, and a Liberty Walk widebody kit. The XRK has great performance. It is top speed surpasses vehicles like the Ray 9 or Desert Crawler, and features launch and early-to-mid acceleration better than of the Stallion. It also has notably excellent braking. However, players may struggle with rough terrain due to its low front suspension, and some players may not be used to its right-hand driver seat. Still, the XRK is an excellent vehicle and one that is recommended for players to own.
